title: Senior Backend Developer
description:
Au sein d’une organisation orientée produit, vous intervenez sur des applications Core Banking critiques en production.
Vous êtes responsable du développement, de l’exploitation et de la modernisation progressive du système d’information, dans une logique d’ownership fort et de responsabilité end-to-end.
Vos missions :
Développement & architecture
Concevoir, développer et maintenir des composants backend du Core Banking LCB.
Intervenir sur un monolithe .NET complexe et critique en production.
Contribuer activement à sa modernisation progressive :
découpage fonctionnel des modules existants,
extraction et design de microservices orientés métier,
adoption d’une architecture hexagonale / clean architecture,
exposition d’APIs REST robustes et scalables.
Modernisation technologique & IA
Utiliser l’IA (LLMs, assistants de dev type GitHub Copilot / Azure Copilot Studio) pour :
accélérer le développement,
automatiser le refactoring,
améliorer la qualité du code,
générer ou maintenir la documentation technique.
Participer à l’adoption de patterns modernes : event-driven, messaging, domain-driven design.
Qualité, production & résilience
Assumer pleinement le cycle de vie des services : you build it, you run it, you fix it.
Garantir la scalabilité, la résilience et la performance des services.
Gérer des flux transactionnels complexes (batch et temps réel).
Mettre en place observabilité, supervision, logs, traçabilité et métriques.
Maintenir un haut niveau de qualité technique (tests unitaires, code review, sécurité).
Participer au run applicatif : support, analyse d’incidents, diagnostics, corrections durables.
Compétences techniques requises
Backend & architecture
Maîtrise avancée de .NET / ASP.NET Core.
Expérience forte sur des architectures monolithiques complexes.
Expérience réelle de transition monolithe → microservices.
Très bonne compréhension des architectures hexagonales / clean architecture.
Maîtrise de la conception et du développement d’APIs REST (Swagger / OpenAPI).
Base de données & messaging
SQL avancé.
PostgreSQL et/ou SQL Server.
ORM : EF Core et/ou NHibernate.
Bonne connaissance des systèmes transactionnels.
Kafka ou technologies de messaging.
DevOps & cloud
CI/CD (Azure DevOps ou équivalent).
Git.
Déploiement applicatif (OpenShift / Kubernetes apprécié).
Observabilité / monitoring / logging.
IA & productivité
Pratique des outils IA appliqués au développement :
utilisation avancée de LLMs,
génération de code,
refactoring assisté,
documentation automatique,
automatisation du testing.
Environnement & organisation
Organisation en squads produit.
Alignement fort entre architecture et organisation (Conway’s Law).
Méthodologies Agile / Scrum.
Outils : Azure DevOps, Git, Jira, Confluence.
Profil recherché:
Développeur Backend expérimenté (minimum 3 ans d’exéprience)
Expérience confirmée en environnements critiques et réglementés.
À l’aise avec le run, le support, la gestion d’incidents et la production.
Pragmatique, responsable et orienté fiabilité.
Capable de moderniser un SI existant.
Curieux et motivé pour intégrer l’IA dans ses pratiques de développement.
Anglais professionnel.

title: Développeur Angular
description:
À propos d’Everfield
Everfield acquiert, développe et accompagne la croissance de sociétés européennes de logiciels B2B spécialisées dans des marchés de niche. Notre mission est d’encourager l’ambition, de soutenir la croissance et de libérer le potentiel de l’écosystème logiciel en Europe.
Les entreprises de l’écosystème Everfield évoluent dans un modèle décentralisé : elles conservent leur marque, leurs équipes et leurs bureaux tout en se concentrant sur ce qu’elles font de mieux — développer leurs produits et accompagner leurs clients. Everfield leur apporte un soutien en recrutement, en ressources humaines, ainsi qu’un accès à des experts en développement et en gestion de sociétés SaaS européennes.
Fondée en 2022, Everfield dispose d’une équipe exceptionnelle d’opérateurs et d’investisseurs logiciels à travers l’Europe.
L’entreprise
Cette opportunité s’inscrit au sein de l’une des sociétés du
portfolio français d’Everfield
, qui développe une plateforme logicielle utilisée par de nombreux acteurs de la
restauration événementielle et du divertissement
.
Forte de plusieurs années d’existence, l’entreprise combine une culture produit solide, une expertise technique, et un esprit d’innovation constant. Elle connaît une croissance soutenue, portée par la digitalisation du parcours client et des opérations sur site.
Dans le cadre de notre développement, nous recherchons un·e
développeur·se Angular expérimenté·e
pour renforcer notre équipe tech et participer à l’évolution de nos interfaces utilisateurs.
Environnement technique
Angular 18+
Laravel 11+
MySQL 8
Docker / NginX
Git
Jira / Slack
Vos missions
Développement et maintenance : Participer activement à la conception, au développement et à l’évolution de nos applications web (interfaces clients, back-office, outils internes).
Tests et qualité : Contribuer à l’écriture de tests unitaires et end-to-end. Participer aux revues de code pour garantir la qualité, la performance et la maintenabilité des solutions livrées.
Documentation : Rédiger et maintenir la documentation technique à jour pour faciliter la compréhension et la transmission des connaissances.
Collaboration interdisciplinaire : Travailler étroitement avec les équipes UX/UI, backend, mobile et QA pour assurer la cohérence et la fluidité des projets.
Veille technologique : Suivre de près les évolutions du framework Angular, les meilleures pratiques de développement front-end et les outils émergents du marché.
Profil recherché
Expérience : Minimum 5 ans d’expérience en développement front-end, dont une solide maîtrise d’Angular (version 13+ minimum, idéalement 18).
Rigueur et curiosité : Vous êtes autonome, rigoureux·se et aimez apprendre en continu.
Esprit d’équipe : Vous appréciez collaborer dans un environnement bienveillant, agile et pluridisciplinaire.
Flexibilité : Vous êtes à l’aise avec le travail hybride (présentiel/remote) et motivé·e par les défis d’une entreprise en forte croissance.
Et si c’était vous ?
Vous souhaitez rejoindre une équipe ambitieuse et conviviale, travailler sur des projets concrets et avoir un impact direct ? Nous serions ravis d’échanger avec vous.
Égalité des chances
Nous sommes un employeur qui respecte l’égalité des chances. Aucun·e candidat·e ne sera soumis·e à un traitement défavorable fondé sur le genre, l’origine, l’âge, l’orientation sexuelle, le handicap ou toute autre caractéristique personnelle.
Toutes nos décisions de recrutement sont fondées sur les compétences, la motivation et le potentiel.

title: Lead Java Developer - Relocation to Portugal

description:
À propos de l’entreprise :
Atlas Energies est une scale-up en hypercroissance qui opère au cœur des piliers de la société moderne : l’énergie, la technologie et la performance.
Portée par une croissance à trois chiffres, notre ambition est claire : transformer durablement la manière dont les entreprises consomment et pilotent leur énergie.
Chaque jour, nous accompagnons PME, ETI et grands comptes pour mieux acheter, mieux consommer et mieux gérer leur énergie, en combinant expertise métier, technologie et excellence humaine.
Ta mission :
Rejoindre Atlas Énergies, c’est intégrer une équipe ambitieuse où l’innovation et la performance sont au cœur de chaque projet. Tu seras un acteur clé de la stratégie commerciale et participeras activement à la croissance du chiffre d’affaires en pilotant des projets de A à Z :
Développement commercial : créer et entretenir des relations solides avec les clients et prospects, identifier de nouvelles opportunités et étendre notre portefeuille.
Stratégie de vente : concevoir des stratégies commerciales pour atteindre les objectifs de l’entreprise.
Conseil personnalisé : présenter des solutions énergétiques adaptées aux besoins spécifiques des clients, en expliquant les avantages des solutions proposées.
Gestion du cycle de vente : de la prospection à la négociation, jusqu’à la signature des contrats, en garantissant un accompagnement client optimal.
Négociation : identifier des conditions contractuelles avantageuses pour nos clients tout en maximisant la rentabilité pour l’entreprise.
Suivi du marché : assurer une veille concurrentielle, analyser les tendances du marché et ajuster la stratégie commerciale en conséquence.
Ce poste offre une évolution rapide, avec des responsabilités croissantes et des possibilités de commissionnement attractives basées sur la performance.
Ton profil :
Autonome et déterminé(e), Tu es animé(e) par la quête de résultats et excelles dans la gestion des relations clients.
Tu as une formation commerciale (idéalement Bac+3 ou supérieur) et une ou plusieurs expériences probantes dans la vente, en particulier en B2B ou dans le secteur de l’énergie.
La vente n’a plus de secret pour toi : tu es un véritable chasseur et tu sais convaincre et négocier.
Tu maîtrises parfaitement le français, à l’écrit comme à l’oral, et tu es capable de communiquer avec aisance et professionnalisme.
Tu es à l’aise avec les outils bureautiques (Word, Excel) et la gestion des chiffres.
Une expérience dans la vente de solutions énergétiques est un atout, mais avant tout, ton savoir-faire commercial et ton approche client feront la différence.
POURQUOI NOUS REJOINDRE ?
Une entreprise en forte croissance : Atlas Énergies offre de nombreuses opportunités d’évolution rapide et un environnement de travail stimulant et bienveillant.
Des avantages attractifs :
Formation continue et accompagnement personnalisé pour favoriser ta montée en compétences.
Ambiance de travail propice à l’autonomie et à l’innovation.
Challenges réguliers et reconnaissance des performances.
INFORMATIONS PRATIQUES
Type de contrat : CDI, temps plein
Horaires : Du lundi au vendredi, de 9h à 18h (40 heures/semaine)
Lieu : Les Berges du Lac II, en présentiel
DÉROULEMENT DES ENTRETIENS

Entretien avec la Talent Acquisition Manager
Rencontre avec le Directeur commercial
Chez Atlas Énergies, nous valorisons l’audace, l’esprit d’initiative et la volonté de réussir. Si tu es motivé(e) par un poste dynamique, avec des perspectives d’évolution et un véritable impact sur le secteur de l’énergie, ce challenge est fait pour toi !
Atlas Énergies réunit l’humain et la technologie pour transformer la performance énergétique des entreprises.
Rejoindre Atlas Énergies, c’est intégrer une équipe en pleine croissance, où l’expertise métier rencontre l’innovation technologique.
Ici, chaque talent compte : tu participes directement à des projets qui ont un impact concret sur la transition énergétique des entreprises.

title: Lead Cloud Platform Engineer
description:
Hi! We're so happy that you're here.
Think-it is a software engineering collective on a mission to leverage technology to regenerate our planet and advance human potential. Our global team helps organisations around the world build digital solutions for a sustainable future.
We partner with brands like BMW, SoundCloud, and Hala Systems, and are core committers to the Eclipse Dataspace Connector (EDC) standard - building sovereign data infrastructure used by some of the largest enterprises in Europe.
Founded in 2017, Think-it is home to a diverse community of changemakers - we represent 10+ nationalities, and 40% of us are women. We're proud to be backed by mission-aligned partners, and our goals are people- and planet-centric.
The Challenge
Think-it is rebuilding its approach to cloud infrastructure and platform engineering. We're not just filling a position - we're defining what excellent looks like. This is your opportunity to shape both our technical infrastructure and our engineering culture from the ground up.
The domain:
Much of our work sits at the intersection of sovereign data exchange and AI. We build infrastructure that enables enterprises to share data across organisational boundaries - with enforceable policies, decentralised trust, and full sovereignty. This means working with the Eclipse Dataspace Connector (EDC), federated architectures, and Kubernetes-native deployments that serve production dataspaces like Catena-X (automotive, 241+ participating companies including BMW, VW, and Mercedes-Benz).
If you're unfamiliar with dataspaces - that's fine. What matters is you're the kind of engineer who can pick up a complex domain, understand the architectural constraints, and build the platform that makes it work reliably.
The mandate:
Establish Think-it as an organisation where every engineer has the knowledge, tools, and confidence to build, deploy, and operate production systems to high standards. You'll own our AWS infrastructure, Kubernetes deployments, and DevOps practices - and make this expertise accessible across the entire engineering team, not locked away in a single person.
You'll work directly with our CTO and Engineering Manager Lead to make architectural decisions that balance technical excellence with business pragmatism. You'll define the standards, establish the practices, and build the culture.
What You'll Do
Own and evolve Think-it's cloud and platform infrastructure.
You'll be responsible for AWS infrastructure, Kubernetes clusters, CI/CD pipelines, observability, and security posture. You'll make architectural decisions and implement solutions that support both client engagements and internal operations.
Build organisational capability, not dependency.
Create documentation that actually gets used. Run workshops. Mentor engineers. The goal: every engineer can confidently prepare production-ready deployments. Build leverage through teaching, not through being the only person who knows how things work.
Engage with stakeholders to translate needs into infrastructure solutions.
Work directly with clients when needed. Present technical approaches to non-technical audiences. Bridge the gap between what stakeholders need and what's technically achievable.
Identify gaps and drive solutions.
When you see something missing - a process, a tool, a skill - work with the CTO and Engineering Manager Lead to address it. Propose solutions and drive change. You'll have input on hiring, skill development, and organisational structure.
Keep the lights on whilst building forward.
Debug production issues. Optimise costs. Improve security. Ensure reliability. Balance operational stability with moving the platform forward.
Invest in your own growth (20% time).
We allocate dedicated time for learning and development. What that looks like is up to you: reading papers, experimenting with new tools, contributing to open source, writing about what you learn, speaking at conferences, or going deep on a technology that expands your capabilities. You own your growth trajectory. We create the space for it.
These responsibilities are a starting point. We're a small company without rigid role boundaries, and we'll help you grow wherever your interests and strengths take you.
Requirements
You'll Thrive Here If
You have deep expertise in cloud and platform engineering.
You've built and operated production systems on AWS. You know Kubernetes beyond kubectl apply - you can deploy, debug, secure, and optimise clusters, and teach others to do the same. You're proficient with infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, Pulumi, or similar). You understand observability, security practices, cost optimisation, and the operational realities of keeping services running.
You care about developer experience.
Whether it's a CLI tool, a deployment pipeline, an internal platform, or API design - you believe the tools engineers use daily should be well-crafted, intuitive, and reduce friction. You think about the person who comes after you.
You've driven change beyond your immediate team.
You've introduced practices, improved processes, or influenced architectural decisions across multiple teams. You can point to specific examples where you made things better for an entire organisation, not just your own backlog.
You communicate clearly and teach effectively.
You explain complex infrastructure concepts to non-technical stakeholders without dumbing things down. You write documentation people actually reference. You present with confidence. You get satisfaction from watching others become more capable.
You take initiative and own outcomes.
You see problems and solve them without waiting for a ticket. You deliver quality work autonomously. When you hit blockers, you find ways around them or escalate with context.
You balance technical excellence with practical constraints.
You pursue quality but know the difference between the right abstraction and over-engineering. You make pragmatic trade-offs and can articulate why.
You work well with distributed, international teams.
You're comfortable with asynchronous communication, different working styles, and building trust across timezones.
You're genuinely aligned with Think-it's mission.
You care about using technology for positive impact and embody our values of Integrity, Collectivism, Ownership, and Fairness in how you work.
You have professional fluency in English
(written and verbal) for international collaboration.
If you don't meet 100% of these, you should still seriously consider applying - at least one of us was missing each of these at the outset!
Tech You'll Work With
This isn't a checklist - it's context on our stack. Depth in some of these matters more than breadth across all of them.
Cloud: AWS (primary), with exposure to multi-cloud patterns
Orchestration: Kubernetes (EKS), Helm, container runtimes
IaC: Terraform (current), open to Pulumi/CDK
CI/CD: GitHub Actions, ArgoCD, or similar GitOps tooling
Observability: Prometheus, Grafana, OpenTelemetry, structured logging
Security: IAM, network policies, secrets management (Vault, KMS), mTLS
Languages: Comfort with at least one of: Go, Python, TypeScript, or Java (for tooling and automation)
Domain-specific: Eclipse Dataspace Connector (EDC) deployments, PostgreSQL, S3 (experience with EDC is a bonus, not a requirement)
Benefits
Life at Think-it
We are explicit about our values, and they shape daily life:
Our
integrity
means we treat trust as our most precious asset and speak truthfully and kindly. Our
collectivism
shows in how we invest in each other's success and approach work as learn-it-alls. Our
ownership
is reflected in how we value impact over effort and proactively create change. Our
fairness
manifests in transparent decisions and commitment to diversity.
What this means practically:
Competitive salary commensurate with experience
Autonomous, remote-friendly culture where you're trusted to do exceptional work
Direct partnership with CTO and Engineering Manager Lead to shape the organisation
20% dedicated time for learning and development
Personal L&D budget
Free weekly team lunches when co-located
Team activities, retreats, and reflections
Globally conscious culture invested in ethical technology
Peer-to-peer learning within a community that values knowledge sharing
We're a distributed team with flexible schedules that support our families and lives outside work. We'd love for you to have some overlap with CET working hours, ideally for at least 4 hours each day!
Think-it is committed to Equal Employment Opportunity regardless of race, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, religion, veteran status, disability, history of disability, or perceived disability.

Participation aux événements organisés par notre équipe : moments conviviaux à ne pas manquer !
Arneo est fier d’être un employeur garantissant l’égalité des chances. Nous nous engageons à promouvoir la diversité au sein de notre communauté et à créer un environnement de travail inclusif. À ce titre, nous accueillons les candidatures de toutes les personnes qualifiées, sans distinction de genre, d’orientation sexuelle, de race, d’ethnie, de croyances, d’âge, de situation familiale, de handicap ou de toute autre caractéristique.

title: Senior Frontend Developer
description:
OoLab est la filiale Tunisienne du groupe Français Oodrive.
Oodrive est la première suite collaborative européenne de confiance fondée en 2002, qui permet à plus d'1 million de personnes de fluidifier leur activité tout en protégeant leurs données sensibles tout en étant l’unique éditeur SaaS qualifié SecNumCloud depuis 2019.
Nous travaillons avec plus de 3500 clients, dont 80% du CAC 40, et nous proposons une suite collaborative avec ces 3 solutions principales :
Oodrive Work : une solution de partage et stockage,
Oodrive Sign : une solution de signature électronique
Oodrive Meet : une solution de dématérialisation de vos réunions de gouvernance
Pour mieux nous connaitre, on vous invite à :
Visiter notre page LinkedIn Oolab
Consulter notre site : Oodrive.com
Ce que vous allez faire au quotidien :
En tant que Senior Frontend Developer, vous travaillerez sur l'une de nos solutions collaboratives. Vous intégrerez une équipe d'une dizaine d'ingénieur.e.s (Back, Front, QA).
Votre rôle au sein de cette équipe
:
Développement :
Vous participerez au développement de nouvelles fonctionnalités, à la maintenance de l’application et à l’amélioration continue de l’architecture front afin de garantir une expérience utilisateur optimale. En collaboration avec l’ensemble de l’équipe Front, juniors comme seniors, vous contribuerez également à faire évoluer le code existant pour en augmenter la qualité, la testabilité et la performance, en cohérence avec les requirements Back.
Participation à la Communauté
: Chez Oodrive on a des Communautés de Pratiques qui réunissent les passionnés autour de certaines thématiques transverses. On a également mis en place des Coding Dojos (Workshops internes avec des experts) et des R&Days (Conférences sur des sujets techniques qui animent la communauté). En participant à ces initiatives, vous deviendrez un acteur de votre progression et de celle de la communauté.
Interactions en interne
: Vous échangez avec l’équipe produit pour prioriser les développements, avec l’équipe Back pour travailler autour des APIs, les QA pour développer et optimiser les tests ainsi qu’avec les autres équipes Front pour partager autour des améliorations, des bonnes pratiques et des dernières technos.
Méthodes et outils
:
Angular, Gitlab, Matrix, JIRA, Confluence.
Comment vous ferez la différence :
Expertise technique
:
Vous avez une forte expérience avec Angular ( 6 ans et plus ).
Vous avez une expérience solide avec les design systems, les librairies partagées Angular, et les architectures modulaires.
Vous appliquez les bonnes pratiques de Clean Architecture et vous savez structurer un front complexe de manière pérenne.
Vous maîtrisez les tests unitaires et intégration côté front.
Legacy
: Autonome et rigoureux·se, avec une excellente capacité d’analyse, vous savez intervenir sur une codebase existante, l’améliorer progressivement et la moderniser lorsque cela a du sens.
Agilité
: Dans nos équipes, on est dans une démarche de développement et de professionnalisation de nos pratiques agiles. Vous êtes sensible à ces approches et force de proposition pour améliorer les processus, les rituels et la qualité globale du delivery.
Nos avantages :
Avantages sociaux : Carte repas, Primes, Assurance maladie,
Conventions : Banque, salle de sport et agence de voyage.
Flexibilité : Planning hybride après la validation de la période d'essai.
Congés payés : Maladie, Maternité et d'autres offerts par la société
Notre process de recrutement :
Echange RH dans nos locaux
Echange technique & pair programming avec l'équipe

title: Salesforce Administrator
description:
SBS works with more than 1,500 banks, building societies and specialized finance providers across more than eighty countries worldwide. We help them to develop, deliver and operationalise their digital transformation strategies. Using our suite of digital banking products and services enables these organisations to deliver remarkable financial services to their clients.
Summary
Job Description
We're looking for a technically-minded Salesforce Administrator to join our growing Revenue Operations
Technology team. You'll handle day-to-day admin tasks such as user management and configuration support, while
contributing to sprint delivery and data quality initiatives. But your real differentiator is technical governance: managing
our source code repository, reviewing developer changes, and acting as the final gatekeeper for production releases.
This role requires understanding Salesforce architecture and development concepts without daily coding. You'll work
cross-functionally in an Agile environment, ensuring technical changes meet our standards before going live.
If you're an ex-developer who prefers orchestrating high-quality releases over writing code all day, this role is for you.
Role Responsibilities
Salesforce Administration & Configuration
Make minor configuration changes directly, and support larger changes delivered via development teams.
Manage user access, profiles, and security in accordance with platform governance policies.
Participate in Agile delivery processes, including backlog refinement, sprint planning, and retrospectives.
Contribute to QA and UAT testing, working with developers to triage bugs and validate fixes.
Support large-scale data updates and imports in collaboration with QA and data quality teams.
Maintain documentation for configuration changes, processes, and standard operating procedures.
Release Management & Source Control
Own the Salesforce source code repository, managing branches, pull requests, and version control.
Review developer-delivered changes to ensure they meet platform standards and follow best practices.
Plan, coordinate, and execute deployments into production.
Act as the final technical checkpoint prior to production releases, ensuring testing, documentation, and rollback
considerations are in place.
Maintain release documentation, deployment logs, and change records.
Stakeholder Engagement & Requirement Gathering
Engage business stakeholders to understand processes, gather and refine requirements, and identify pain points.
Translate business needs into structured requirements suitable for estimation and development.
Collaborate with developers to validate scope, clarify requirements, and ensure delivery aligns with business intent.
Provide functional oversight and input into technical solution design discussions.
Platform Support & Cross-System Awareness
Support the operation of integrated platforms and tools (e.g. Gong, Power BI, etc.) by understanding data flows and
dependencies.
Assist in troubleshooting data issues across systems and coordinating with relevant platform owners.
Contribute to testing and validation of end-to-end changes that span multiple systems
Qualifications
Role Requirements
Essential
Salesforce Administrator certification (ADM201).
5+ years of experience as a Salesforce Administrator or in a similar functional/technical CRM support role.
Developer background or experience: you don't need to code daily, but you must understand Apex, APIs, and platform architecture
Strong experience with source control and release management technologies (e.g. Git, change sets, CI/CD tools).
Proven ability to gather requirements from stakeholders and translate them into technical specifications.
Experience working in Agile environments with developers and cross-functional teams.
Able to assess whether solutions use appropriate declarative or programmatic tools based on business needs and
best practices.
Excellent attention to detail and documentation habits.
Comfortable being the "gatekeeper" who enforces production standards.
Desirable
Advanced Administrator (ADM211) or Platform App Builder certification.
Platform Developer I certification.
Experience in RevOps or B2B SaaS environments.
Hands on knowledge of Apex, SOQL, and API integrations
Familiarity with Salesforce Inspector, Data Loader, or Workbench.
Experience with Salesforce Service Cloud.
Familiarity with test automation in Salesforce.
Experience with data quality initiatives, governance frameworks, or cross-system reconciliation
Additional Information
About Working With Us
We value technical excellence and collaborative problem-solving. Our team works in a fast-paced, Agile environmentwhere your input shapes how we build and deliver solutions. You'll have autonomy to make decisions while working alongside talented professionals and engaged business stakeholders.
We're committed to continuous improvement, knowledge sharing, and building systems that scale. If you're someone who gets excited about clean deployments, well-documented processes, and elegant solutions, you'll fit right in.

title: Senior Backend Developer (ROR)
description:
A Senior Backend Developer (Ruby on Rails) is responsible for designing, developing, and testing server-side logic for web applications using Ruby and the Ruby on Rails framework. They build and maintain efficient, reusable, and reliable Ruby code, optimize the system for speed and scalability, and ensure high performance and availability of the applications. Collaborating with cross-functional teams, they integrate front-end elements into applications, conduct code reviews, mentor junior developers, and translate business requirements into technical solutions. The ideal candidate holds a Bachelor's or Master's degree in a relevant field and has at least eight years of experience with Ruby on Rails. They are proficient in various software testing methodologies, possess strong problem-solving skills, and have excellent English fluency.
Essential Duties and Responsibilities:
Develops software solutions by studying information needs, conferring with users, studying systems flow, data usage, and work processes; investigating problem areas; and following the software development lifecycle.
Designing, developing, and testing server-side logic by developing high-quality, reusable, and scalable code using Ruby on Rails and other advanced backend technologies.
Documents and demonstrates solutions by developing documentation, flowcharts, layouts, diagrams, charts, code comments, and clear code.
Build and maintain efficient, reusable, and reliable Ruby code. Improve and optimize the performance of existing components and systems.
Updates job knowledge by studying state-of-the-art development tools, programming techniques, and computing equipment, and by participating in educational opportunities, reading professional publications, maintaining personal networks, and participating in professional organizations.
Identify and resolve performance and scalability issues by constantly looking for ways to improve the software by identifying bottlenecks and bugs, and devising solutions to these problems.
Lead and collaborate with agile cross-functional teams by collaborating with product management, UX designers, and frontend developers to define and implement innovative backend solutions, optimizing user interaction and experience.
Code reviews and mentorship by conducting peer code reviews, providing mentorship, and sharing knowledge with team members to maintain high code quality standards.
Understand business requirements and translate them into technical requirements by working closely with project managers and other stakeholders to understand the business goals and objectives and to understand and resolve complex business issues.
Implement automated testing and unit tests by working closely with the QA team to build and maintain automated tests to ensure quality and save time and effort in testing.
Improves operations by conducting systems analysis and recommending changes in policies and procedures.
Accomplishes engineering and organization mission by completing related results as needed.
Qualifications:
Education: A Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.
Experience: Minimum of 8 years of experience as a Backend Developer, including 3+ years of hands-on experience with Ruby.
Project Management: Experience with agile project management and workflow tools such as Jira, Asana, Trello, or similar.
Version Control: Proficiency in using Git or other version control systems.
Competencies:
Technical Skills:
Proficient in Ruby, Ruby on Rails, and other relevant backend technologies.
Experience with database technologies like PostgreSQL, MySQL, and NoSQL databases.
Familiarity with RESTful API design and development.
Knowledge of user authentication and authorization mechanisms.
Experience with cloud services like AWS, Google Cloud, or Azure.
Understanding of fundamental design principles behind a scalable application.
Familiarity with continuous integration and deployment tools.
Ability to understand business requirements and translate them into technical requirements.
A knack for benchmarking and optimization.
Software Design: Demonstrated proficiency in software design principles and patterns. The ability to design efficient and reusable backend systems that drive complex web applications.
Software Testing: Proficiency in various types of software testing methodologies, including unit testing, functional testing, integration testing, and regression testing. Familiarity with testing frameworks.
Problem-Solving Skills: Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills. Ability to troubleshoot complex software issues.
Communication Skills: Excellent written and verbal communication skills. The ability to communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders is crucial.
Teamwork and Leadership: Proven ability to lead and work as part of a team. Ability to mentor and guide junior developers.
Attention to Detail: High attention to detail with a passion for delivering high-quality user experiences.
Time Management: Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage priorities and time effectively.
Adaptability: The ability to quickly learn and adapt to new technologies and programming tools. Openness to receiving and acting on feedback, with the ultimate goal of continuous improvement.
Continuous Learning: A commitment to learning and keeping up with the latest trends and best practices in backend development.
User-Centric: A strong focus on user experiences and outcomes. Knowledge of UX principles and best practices.
Fluency in English: Excellent command of English language, both written and spoken. This is necessary for clear, effective communication.
Flexibility in Work Schedule: Willingness and ability to adjust work schedule as necessary to collaborate with team members in different time zones, meet project deadlines, or address urgent issues.

title: End of Studies Internship - BI Engineer Intern - IS&IT
description:
About Us
At Medius, we believe managing finance should be about strategy, not stress. That same mindset shapes not only the solutions we build, but also the culture we create for our people. We remove complexity, embrace innovation, and give our teams the freedom to focus on what truly matters — whether that’s transforming the future of finance with AI or finding balance to go home on time.
Founded in Sweden in 2001, Medius has grown from a local startup into a global leader in cloud-based spend management solutions. Today, thousands of organizations worldwide trust us to simplify accounts payable and spend management processes. Our journey has been driven by continuous innovation, a passion for technology, and above all — the people who make it happen.
We’re more than a software company. We’re a team of problem-solvers, innovators and collaborators working together to reinvent the category of accounts payable. Our solutions use Artificial Intelligence to eliminate manual work, bring clarity, confidence, and control, and empower finance teams of the future.
At Medius,
our values guide how we work and grow together:
Connect – We believe in the power of people—individually and collectively—and our success depends on understanding and respecting each other. We appreciate that ‘empowering finance teams of the future’ is an exciting endeavor, and we share it with everyone around us.
Question – We enjoy the challenge of our work and the thrill of collaboration. We are not afraid to question ourselves and each other because we believe diverse perspectives can lead to better outcomes and that there is great power in resolution.
Own – We are thorough, thoughtful, and decisive. We anticipate what’s next, what a customer might need, and then we deliver. That’s how we get things done. And that’s how we remain a leader. Customers trust us to do our job so that they can focus on what they do best.
At Medius, you’ll join a diverse, global community where curiosity is celebrated, ideas matter, and innovation never stops. If you’re passionate about technology, eager to make an impact, and ready to grow alongside a team that lives its values, Medius is where you can do your best work — your impact is global.
Learn more at www.medius.com
Role Summary
We are looking for a BI Developer (End of Studies Intern, 6 months) to join our IS&IT Data Integration team. You will lead the migration of existing Domo assets—dataflows, datasets, dashboards, and reports—to Microsoft Fabric (Power BI), ensuring robust data modeling, performance, governance, and user experience. You’ll collaborate with business stakeholders and technical teams to deliver trusted, scalable BI that supports decision-making across Medius.
What you’ll do:
Inventory and assess current Domo assets (dataflows, datasets, dashboards/reports), including dependencies, transformations, schedules, and usage.
Design and implement target-state data models in Microsoft Fabric/Power BI (star schema, data marts) with performance, maintainability, and governance in mind.
Rebuild and enhance dashboards/reports in Power BI, ensuring parity with Domo while improving UX, consistency, and performance.
Develop ETL/ELT dataflows and pipelines in Fabric (e.g., Dataflows Gen2, Pipelines), and configure refresh, lineage, and monitoring.
Implement security and governance (e.g., RLS, workspace structure, naming conventions, documentation standards).
Optimize DAX and Power Query (M) for incremental refresh, and efficient dataset design.
Validate end-to-end data accuracy with robust testing plans; manage stakeholder sign-off.
Produce clear technical and user documentation; deliver handover and enablement sessions for end users and support teams.
Contribute to continuous improvement and BI best practices across the IS&IT organization.
Technologies and environment
Microsoft Fabric (Power BI, Dataflows Gen2, OneLake, Pipelines)
Domo (dataflows, datasets, dashboards)
DAX, Power Query (M), SQL
Optional/plus: Python, REST APIs, Azure services
What You’ll Bring
Final-year student in Computer Science, Data/BI, Information Systems, Engineering, or related field (eligible for a 6-month end-of-studies internship).
Hands-on experience with Power BI (data modeling, DAX, Power Query) and solid SQL skills.
Understanding of ETL/ELT concepts, dimensional modeling (star/snowflake), and the BI lifecycle.
Familiarity with data governance (RLS, documentation, naming/versioning) and dashboard UX.
Strong analytical thinking, problem-solving, and communication skills; ability to collaborate with both business and technical audiences.
Proficiency in English.
Preferred: Exposure to Microsoft Fabric beyond Power BI (e.g., Pipelines, OneLake); knowledge of sales, finance, and marketing metrics and reporting; experience with agile tools (Jira/Confluence); scripting with Python or working with REST APIs.
Please send your resume in English (resumes in French will be automatically rejected)
.

title: Senior Software Engineer
description:
Join the team redefining the future of global living.
At
The Flex
, we believe renting a home should feel instant, intelligent, and effortless — as seamless as booking a ride.
Our ambition is bold: enabling anyone to live anywhere, anytime, without friction.
Powered by
Base360.ai
, our proprietary automation engine, we’re building the operating system for modern renting — connecting property data, orchestrating operations, and enabling seamless stays across continents.
If you’re motivated by
complex systems, automation, and building software that operates in the real world
, this role gives you the ownership and scope to make a visible, lasting impact.
💡 What You’ll Build
As a
Senior Software Engineer
, you’ll design, build, and scale critical parts of The Flex platform — from core services and APIs to automation workflows and real-time systems.
You’ll operate with high autonomy, owning problems end-to-end and contributing directly to architectural decisions, while staying deeply hands-on with production code.
This role is for engineers who want
impact, not meetings
— and who take pride in building systems that are reliable, elegant, and scalable.
⚙️ Your Mission
Build Core Systems
Develop and maintain scalable services that power bookings, payments, availability, pricing, and guest experience.
Design Robust APIs
Create clean, well-structured APIs that connect Base360.ai with internal tools and external partners.
Automate Operations
Build event-driven workflows that eliminate manual processes and unlock operational leverage.
Ship with Confidence
Deploy and operate cloud-native infrastructure on AWS with a focus on reliability, security, and performance.
Solve Meaningful Problems
Work on real-time booking synchronization, pricing intelligence, keyless access logic, AI-powered alerts, and live operational dashboards.
Collaborate for Impact
Work closely with product, data, and operations teams to turn complex requirements into simple, effective systems.
🧠 You’re a Great Fit If You Have
Strong experience building production systems with Node.js, React, and AWS
A solid understanding of distributed systems and API-first design
Experience with Python / FastAPI for automation or data services (bonus)
A track record of shipping clean, maintainable, scalable code
Comfort owning features and systems from design to production
Curiosity about automation, AI-driven operations, and proptech
A bias toward execution — you deliver, iterate, and improve continuously
🌍 Why You’ll Love Working Here
Visible Impact
Your work will directly affect thousands of stays and real-world operations.
Autonomy & Trust
You’ll own systems and decisions without unnecessary process or micromanagement.
Fast Growth Environment
We move quickly, learn constantly, and value technical excellence.
Performance-Based Rewards
Competitive compensation with upside for high performers.
Remote-First
Work from anywhere — outcomes matter more than hours.
🚫 This Role Is Not for You If
You want a narrowly scoped role with limited ownership
You avoid production responsibility
You’re uncomfortable working in fast-moving environments
“Good enough” is your standard

title: Webmaster
description:
Location: Tunis, Tunisia (Hybrid)
Department: Global Marketing

About Questel

With over 1800 employees worldwide, Questel serves thousands of customers across industries with tools that accelerate innovation and strategic decision-making.
Position Overview

You act as the bridge between marketing, IT and external partners to guarantee a smooth, secure, high-performance and geo-optimized web presence.
Key Responsibilities
Monitor and ensure the smooth technical operation of various websites (anomaly detection, performance monitoring, reporting).
Assist with content integration and localization (text, images, videos, products, etc.) on web platform for marketing and regional campaigns
Develop and assist in creating landing pages to support marketing campaigns and GEO-targeted efforts
Efficiently manage and prioritize technical incidents and requests using Jira (or similar ticketing system), ensuring clear communication, timely resolution, or proper escalation.
Implement technical SEO corrections (tags, redirects, structure, performance, indexing) and integrate GEO best-practices (structured data) in collaboration with our SEO expert.
Continuous Improvement
Contribute to the evolution of our web platforms by researching and advocating for new technologies, tools, and best practices.
Participate in QA testing for new features, site migrations, and platform updates.

title: Senior Software Engineer
description:
The Senior Full Stack Software Engineer works closely with Business Analysts, Product Owner, Product Managers, QA and Support teams to develop new functionality and provide an excellent experience for our customers and partners. Every member of the team plays a key role in maintaining the team's culture and benefits from an environment of collaboration and support.
The successful candidate participates in the development of new software features, establishes new integrations, solves issues, and supports mutiple modernizations of the product. At this level, it’s expected the engineer can provide mentorship to less experienced team members within our Agile Scrum teams, and collaborate on architecture and design efforts.
Essential Duties:
* Working within our agile scrum teams, alongside product owner, business analyst, developers, QA, scrum master.
* Our teams are globally located and you may be working in teams with individuals from different time zones.
* Leading the development of new features spanning client, server, and cloud components of the application stack.
* Integrating third party libraries and services into the existing application.
* Investigating customer-reported software issues, security vulnerabilities and improvements to resolve in a timely and effective manner.
* Involvement in all aspects of our Agile process including requirements analysis, backlog grooming, estimating, planning, daily standups, and retrospectives.
* Writing UML in the form of class, sequence, flow, and activity diagrams.
* Mentoring and supporting less experienced developers, both directly with code review and indirectly with advice and guidance.
* Supporting the product owner in understanding the complexity of work, identifying constraints and dependencies and in estimating delivery timescales.
* Working with architects and principal engineers to design features and functionality and to help to articulate the future direction of JavaScript, Java, and Cloud based software development, design principles and architecture.
* Writing unit and integration tests for both client and server features using the most appropriate testing frameworks such as JUnit, Jest, Selenium, and Cucumber.
Basic Qualifications:
* Strong Java software development (Java 8, J2EE), with in-depth understanding of the language.
* Knowledge of modern JavaScript frameworks/libraries, JQuery, HTML and CSS.
*  Experience working with the Spring framework and its ecosystem which is used extensively in our teams.
* Strong OOP with some UML/Design Pattern exposure.
*  Experience working in an Agile (pref. Scrum) and iterative development approach.
*  Strong written and verbal communication skills in English.
* Enthusiasm and ability to collaborate well with others, including remote teams
* Professional pride, drive and curiosity, a diligent self-starter that keeps up to date with the best practices and keeps your skill set sharp.
*  Strong problem resolution skills
Preferred Qualifications:
* Deep knowledge of AWS services including but not limited to EC2, ECR, ECS, RDS, S3, DynamoDB, Lambda, API Gateway, SNS, SQS, Pinpoint, Event Bridge
* Experience with our toolchain: Docker, IntelliJ IDEA, JUnit, Selenium, Ant, Maven, Git, Jira, Vue.js, Hibernate, PostgreSQL
* Familiarity with design tools and principals and a desire to work with customers to describe their ideal user interfaces and experiences.
* Strong ability to think through large abstract systems and processes, comfortable problem-solving and working with data.
* Exposure to data analytics and reporting technologies with an understanding of database optimization and performance tuning.
*  Awareness of security related best practices and the process of identifying, triaging, and resolving vulnerabilities within an application stack.
About Infor
Infor is where ambition meets impact. Join a global community of bold thinkers and innovators, where your expertise doesn’t just solve problems. it shapes industries, unlocks opportunities, and creates real-world impact for billions of people. At Infor, you’re not just building a career. you’re helping to build what’s next.
Infor is a global leader in business cloud software products for companies in industry specific markets. Infor builds complete industry suites in the cloud and efficiently deploys technology that puts the user experience first, leverages data science, and integrates easily into existing systems. Over 60,000 organizations worldwide rely on Infor to help overcome market disruptions and achieve business-wide digital transformation.

We have a relentless commitment to a culture based on PBM™. Informed by the principles that allow a free and open society to flourish, PBM™ prepares individuals to innovate, improve, and transform while fostering a healthy, growing organization that creates long-term value for its clients and supporters and fulfillment for its employees.

title: Golang Full Stack Developer
description:
We are one of the leading technology groups in Italy. We operate as a Business & Technology Accelerator for companies, financial institutions, and public sector organizations, supporting the evolution of their IT and digital strategies. Through an ecosystem of companies that share values, business strategies, and a common vision, we enable our clients to unlock the full potential of technology, amplifying its positive impact on both the economy and society.
What To Expect
We are a leading cyber security consultancy and Continuous Controls Monitoring (CCM) provider dedicated to helping organisations protect their digital assets and infrastructure. Our innovative solutions, expert consultancy and bespoke CCM services are designed to meet the unique security needs of businesses across various industries.
The role provides a fundamental part of the Quod Orbis team.
As a Developer at our company, you will be involved in the full software development lifecycle, from concept and design to testing and deployment. You will work closely with our team of developers, product managers, and cyber security experts to create high-quality, scalable, and secure SaaS solutions. The Developer will have a strong background in software development, with a passion for technology and a keen interest in cyber security.
What You Will Do
Drive the technical implementation of the move from single-tenant to multi-tenant architecture.
Work closely with the Head of Platform to plan, design, and execute the migration strategy.
Develop tenant-aware backend services and APIs in Go.
Support data migration, including schema changes, backfills, and transition from existing environments.
Implement and test tenant isolation, access control, and configuration management.
Update frontend components and routing to support tenant context where needed.
Improve database performance, partitioning, and query efficiency for multi-tenant systems.
Enhance observability, logging, and monitoring with tenant-level visibility.
Collaborate with DevOps to achieve zero-downtime releases and safe rollouts.
Mentor backend developers and promote best practices across the team (SAST/DAST).
What We Expect From You
Contact with others (face-to-face, by telephone or otherwise, including visits to other sites)
Attention to detail and a high level of accuracy
6+ years of backend development experience.
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field. A Master’s degree or relevant certifications are a desirable but not mandatory.
Strong Go development experience, ideally in distributed or high-scale systems.
Solid frontend experience.
Proven experience with multi-tenant system design or migration projects.
Deep understanding of tenancy models; database per tenant, schema per tenant, or shared schema and the trade-offs of each.
Skilled in data modelling, schema partitioning, and tenant-aware querying.
Experience implementing tenant-based authentication, authorisation, and RBAC.
Experience in migrating legacy applications or environments into shared, multi-tenant platforms.
Comfortable working with relational databases (PostgreSQL, MariaDB, or MySQL).
Strong collaboration and communication skills — able to partner with backend, frontend, and DevOps teams effectively.
What We Offer You
A leadership role at the helm of a dynamic and innovative cyber security SaaS company.
The opportunity to shape the future of cyber security through cutting-edge technology and services.
Competitive compensation package.
A collaborative and supportive culture that values innovation, integrity, and inclusivity

Amaris Consulting
is an independent technology consulting firm providing guidance and solutions to businesses. With more than 1,000 clients across the globe, we have been rolling out solutions in major projects for over a decade – this is made possible by an international team of 7,600 people spread across 5 continents and more than 60 countries. Our solutions focus on four different Business Lines: Information System & Digital, Telecom, Life Sciences and Engineering. We’re focused on building and nurturing a top talent community where all our team members can achieve their full potential. Amaris is your steppingstone to cross rivers of change, meet challenges and achieve all your projects with success.
At Amaris, we strive to provide our candidates with the best possible recruitment experience. We like to get to know our candidates, challenge them, and be able to give them proper feedback as quickly as possible. Here's what our recruitment process looks like:
Brief Call
: Our process typically begins with a brief virtual/phone conversation to get to know you! The objective? Learn about you, understand your motivations, and make sure we have the right job for you!
Interviews
(the average number of interviews is 3 - the number may vary depending on the level of seniority required for the position). During the interviews, you will meet people from our team: your line manager of course, but also other people related to your future role. We will talk in depth about you, your experience, and skills, but also about the position and what will be expected of you. Of course, you will also get to know Amaris: our culture, our roots, our teams, and your career opportunities!
Case study
: Depending on the position, we may ask you to take a test. This could be a role play, a technical assessment, a problem-solving scenario, etc.
As you know, every person is different and so is every role in a company. That is why we have to adapt accordingly, and the process may differ slightly at times. However, please know that we always put ourselves in the candidate's shoes to ensure they have the best possible experience.
We look forward to meeting you!

title: Senior Backend Engineer (Security & FinTech)
description:
We are one of the leading technology groups in Italy. We operate as a Business & Technology Accelerator for companies, financial institutions, and public sector organizations, supporting the evolution of their IT and digital strategies. Through an ecosystem of companies that share values, business strategies, and a common vision, we enable our clients to unlock the full potential of technology, amplifying its positive impact on both the economy and society.
What To Expect
We are looking for a “Security Champion” to join our Product Engineering team. If you are a “FinTech Survivor” who loves strict code reviews and “Zero Trust” environments but is tired of the slow pace of traditional banking, this role is for you. You won’t just be patching vulnerabilities; you will design the secure architecture that protects our customers’ most sensitive financial data.
As a Senior Backend Engineer, you will act as the “Anchor” for our FinTech domain. You will own the server-side logic with a specific mandate: ensure our system is secure by design. You will build the “secure pipes” that deliver data to our AI models, ensuring compliance with federal audits (SOC2, GLBA) without slowing down innovation.
What You Will Do
You will design systems where no component trusts another by default, defining strict encryption standards (at rest/in transit) and enforcing data segregation between tenants.
You will write code that mitigates OWASP Top 10 risks (SQLi, XSS) by design, rather than fixing them after the fact.
You will implement robust Authentication and fine-grained Authorization mechanisms using protocols like OAuth2, OIDC, and RBAC/ABAC.
You are responsible for building immutable logging systems where every API call and database access is traced and searchable to satisfy federal audits.
You will build data anonymization pipelines that strip PII from banking data before it reaches our Data Scientists, ensuring they can work safely.
You will proactively update dependencies and refactor code based on security scanning results (SAST/DAST).
What We Expect From You
5+ years of experience in backend development.
A strong track record in regulated industries (FinTech, HealthTech, Insurance) or cybersecurity engineering.
Proficiency in designing Microservices, Event-Driven architectures, or Modular Monoliths.
Expert skills in API Design (REST, GraphQL, gRPC) and Testing Frameworks.
Deep knowledge of Application Security (OWASP Top 10), Cryptography standards, and Identity Protocols (OAuth2, OIDC, SAML).
Someone used to strict code reviews and handling PII, but looking for a faster startup environment.
A developer who naturally gravitates towards security tickets and always spots the XSS vulnerability in code reviews.
Experience building audit trails for SOC2 or GDPR.

title: Technical Product Manager
description:
This position is 100% remote.
What are we looking for?
CobbleWeb
, a leading developer of custom online marketplaces, is looking for an ambitious mid-career
Product Manager
who would like to grow their skill-set and expertise by working on more complex and challenging e-commerce projects.

This role is a good fit for someone who can balance previous methodologies and insights with new ways of working. A
capacity and willingness to adapt
means that you will be adept at learning from delegated tasks and feedback, supported by some coaching on the more complex marketplace-related aspects.
As a critical lynchpin, you are skilled at
cross-functional collaboration
. In conjunction with clients and technical teams, you enjoy designing optimal solutions by analysing, discussing, and aligning business goals with product specifications that are technically feasible and user-friendly.
You are a curious leader who
interrogates all aspects
of the product development process to identify potential improvements or resolve potential conflicts. You believe that
information-sharing
is a valuable tool for teams to shape best-in-class solutions.
If this is you,
CobbleWeb
may be a good fit:
An independent and profitable business that is doubling in size.
More than 12 years of experience in designing, building, and optimising high-growth online marketplaces.
Technically complex projects often present unique business challenges that need solving.
A distributed company, headquartered in London, UK, with a strong documentation and iteration culture that supports remote team members based in Europe, Africa, the Middle East, and the Caucasus region.
We take full ownership of marketplace projects in close collaboration with our clients. We’re a close-knit in-house team who love working together. We don’t outsource core functions.
Work-life balance
Team support
Career development
Job fulfilment
Innovative projects
Responsibilities
Discovery and Planning Phase:
Collaborate with clients to understand their business goals and user expectations.
Advocate for a lean approach, driving principles such as formulating and testing assumptions, shipping often, and incorporating user feedback.
Gain client buy-in through effective use of written documents, user flow diagrams, wireframes, and design mockups.
Evaluate and propose best-in-class solutions, understanding the pros and cons of different approaches.
Document and update functional specifications based on feedback and contributions from team members.
Development Phase
Maintain a healthy product backlog, prioritising tasks based on factors such as budget, return on investment, and product-market fit.
Monitor project budgets, identifying potential overspending and recommending adjustments to scope or specifications.
Ensure development aligns with documented specifications and assist the testing team with test case preparation.

Experience in managing and collaborating with multiple stakeholders.
Proficiency with diagramming and project management tools (e.g., Jira, Confluence).
Familiarity with Agile principles, Lean startup methodology, and the platform economy.

Proven ability to deliver projects within meticulously planned scopes, budgets,s and time frames, manage risk, and avoid bottlenecks.
Strong interpersonal and collaborative skills.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills.

User-centric and business-goal focused.
A big-picture thinker who doesn’t lose sight of the details.
Highly organised, thorough, analytical, and deadline-focused.
Consistent in your execution of tasks.
Uphold high levels of productivity by not reinventing the wheel.

Linedata est éditeur mondial de logiciels, fournisseur de services et données à valeur ajoutée dédié au secteur financier. Linedata accompagne ses clients gestionnaires d’actifs, traditionnels ou alternatifs, administrateurs de fonds et institutions de crédits et financements, dans leurs défis, grâce à des solutions innovantes et efficaces.
Avec plus de 25 ans d’expérience dans le domaine de la FinTech, Linedata propose à ses équipes tout au long de leur carrière la possibilité de construire une forte expertise alliant finance et technologie dans un environnement international stimulant.

Parce que nous sommes indépendants, fiables, accessibles et engagés, parce que nos équipes sont soudées, à l'écoute, attentionnées et passionnées, parce que nos experts sont à la fois des partenaires spécialisés et attentifs, nous nous surpassons pour créer des solutions globales de leadership.
Stimulés par un environnement en transformation et par nos clients qui ont besoin de s’y adapter, enthousiasmés par les entreprises collaboratives partageant notre esprit entrepreneurial et nos ambitions, nous révélons leur puissance créative pour faire circuler l'argent et alimenter l'économie, nous aidons les gens à développer leurs capacités et leur dignité pour les générations futures.
Contexte du poste
Dans le cadre de sa croissance,
Linedata
, éditeur de référence dans le domaine de l’Asset Management et les crédits & Financement destinés aux
institutions financières
, recherche un
Data Scientist / ML Engineer
passionné par la donnée et l’intelligence artificielle.
Vous intégrerez une équipe déjà en place, qui collabore avec les experts métiers et les équipes de développement, pour concevoir, mettre en œuvre et industrialiser des modèles analytiques au service de la performance et de la prise de décision.
Job Requirement
Missions principales
Concevoir, développer et déployer des modèles de machine learning et d’IA générative répondant aux besoins des métiers financiers ;
Analyser et valoriser les données internes et externes afin de générer des insights exploitables ;
Participer à la constitution et à la structuration des data pipelines en collaboration avec les Data Engineers ;
Contribuer à la mise en place de solutions d’industrialisation et de monitoring des modèles ;
Collaborer étroitement avec les équipes produit et les experts métiers pour traduire les besoins fonctionnels en solutions data ;
Réaliser des études exploratoires, prototypes et POC pour valider les approches analytiques ;
Assurer une veille technologique sur les nouvelles méthodes et outils d’analyse de données.
Compétences techniques requises
Maîtrise des langages de programmation Python (pandas, scikit-learn, PyTorch, TensorFlow, etc.) ;
Expérience professionnelle en prompt engineering.
Solide compréhension des techniques de machine learning, deep learning et statistiques appliquées ;
Bonne connaissance des bases de données SQL / NoSQL et des environnements cloud (AWS, Azure, GCP) ,
Connaissance des bonnes pratiques d’industrialisation (Git, CI/CD, APIs, Docker, k8s etc.) ;
La connaissance du domaine financier (marchés, risques, conformité, asset management, etc.) est un atout majeur.
Profil recherché
Diplôme d’ingénieur ou Master (Bac+5) en data science, mathématiques appliquées, informatique, statistiques ou équivalent ;
Expérience de 2 à 5 ans minimum dans un poste similaire (une première expérience dans le secteur financier est un plus) ;
Bonne maîtrise des modèles NLP / IA générative open source (manage services et déploiement local) ;
Capacité à déployer des modèles sur GPU/Kuda
Solides capacités analytiques et rigueur scientifique ;
Esprit d’équipe, sens du service et excellente communication ;
Curiosité technologique et envie de travailler dans un environnement innovant et collaboratif.

Elle fait partie intégrante de notre façon de travailler et est impérative pour notre succès. :

Diplômé(e) d’une école d’ingénieur, master en IA, data science ou informatique
0 à 2 ans d'expérience (stage ou projet significatif accepté)
Un portfolio, un projet GitHub ou un Kaggle est un plus

title: Senior Generative AI (Gen AI) Engineer
description:
Senior Generative AI (GenAI) Engineer
Description
InnovationTeam
is a forward-thinking technology company that specializes in delivering advanced AI-driven solutions to enterprises and government organizations. We are currently seeking a highly skilled Senior Generative AI (GenAI) Engineer to join our AI team.
As a Senior GenAI Engineer at
InnovationTeam
, you will be responsible for designing, developing, and deploying large-scale generative AI solutions. You will work closely with cross-functional teams to translate business and technical requirements into robust AI systems and bring them into production.
This role requires deep expertise in modern AI architectures, large language models, and production-ready software engineering practices. The ideal candidate is highly motivated, detail-oriented, and capable of owning AI solutions end to end.
At
InnovationTeam
, we value innovation, collaboration, and continuous learning. We provide a flexible and inclusive work environment where your expertise is recognized and opportunities for professional growth are continuously supported.
Role Overview
We are seeking a skilled AI Engineer with a strong focus on Generative AI technologies to develop, optimize, and deploy AI Agents and solutions. The role involves working on real-world use cases and data analytics problems and delivering production-ready AI systems.
Key Responsibilities
Design, develop, and optimize Generative AI solutions (LLMs, RAG systems, AI agents)
Fine-tune, evaluate, and deploy large language models for enterprise use cases
Build and maintain end-to-end AI pipelines, from data ingestion to inference
Implement Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) using vector databases and knowledge stores
Integrate GenAI solutions with existing systems via APIs and microservices
Optimize model performance, latency, and cost on cloud platforms (OCI, AWS, Azure, or GCP)
Collaborate with product, data, and engineering teams to translate business needs into AI solutions
Ensure AI solutions follow best practices in security, governance, and responsible AI
Stay up to date with the latest developments in GenAI, LLMs, and AI tooling
Required Skills & Qualifications
Minimum 5 years of professional experience in AI / Machine Learning
Master's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, or a related field
Proficiency in programming languages such as python , Java , and Ruby
Strong experience with Large Language Models (LLMs) (e.g., GPT, LLaMA, Mistral, etc.)
Hands-on experience with Python and AI/ML frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow)
Solid understanding of NLP, deep learning, and transformer architectures
Experience with RAG architectures, vector databases (FAISS, OpenSearch, Pinecone, etc.)
Strong software engineering skills (APIs, REST, microservices, Docker, Kubernetes)
Experience deploying AI workloads on cloud platforms (OCI preferred)
Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills
Excellent command of English (written and spoken)
Nice to Have
Experience with GPU-based training and inference
Familiarity with MLOps / LLMOps practices
Experience in regulated or enterprise environments
Knowledge of AI governance, ethics, and compliance frameworks
What We Offer
Opportunity to work on cutting-edge Generative AI projects
Collaborative, innovation-driven environment
Competitive compensation package
Access to modern AI infrastructure and cloud resources
Professional growth and learning opportunities

title: Ingénieur en Intelligence Artificielle
description:
Umanlink Group Carrière Ingénieur en Intelligence Artificielle
Lieu : Tunis
Expérience : minimum 5 ans
Type de contrat : CDI / Freelance
Rejoignez une équipe d’experts visionnaires, où l’intelligence artificielle façonne des projets innovants et impactants. Participez à des missions captivantes tout en profitant d’opportunités d’évolution et de collaboration internationale dans un cadre dynamique et inspirant.
Vos Défis
Concevoir et développer des modèles d’intelligence artificielle pour résoudre des problématiques complexes.
Travailler sur des projets de Machine Learning, Deep Learning, et NLP.
Optimiser et déployer des algorithmes sur des environnements cloud ou embarqués.
Analyser et structurer des ensembles de données pour entraîner des modèles performants.
Collaborer avec les équipes pour intégrer des solutions IA dans les produits existants.
Vos Super-pouvoirs
Solides compétences en Python et frameworks IA (TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn).
Expertise en traitement de données, big data et outils comme Spark ou Hadoop.
Expérience dans les environnements cloud (AWS SageMaker, Azure ML, GCP AI).
Connaissance des techniques avancées en Deep Learning (CNN, RNN, GANs).

title: Senior Data Scientist
description:
We are one of the leading technology groups in Italy. We operate as a Business & Technology Accelerator for companies, financial institutions, and public sector organizations, supporting the evolution of their IT and digital strategies. Through an ecosystem of companies that share values, business strategies, and a common vision, we enable our clients to unlock the full potential of technology, amplifying its positive impact on both the economy and society.
What To Expect
We are looking for a Data Scientist capable of bridging the gap between vague business questions and rigorous mathematical answers. You must be capable of digging into messy data to find the “truth” and defining what problems should (and can) be solved. You will own the scientific strategy, determining which problems are solvable and proving that our solutions actually work.
As our Senior Data Scientist, you will act as the Strategic Lead of the Applied Science Track. You will report to the Head of Engineering/Principal Scientist and define the roadmap for our Machine Learning, NLP, and Optimization (Operations Research) initiatives.
Crucially, this is a hands-on leadership role. You will be the "Architect" who defines the problem, but you will also actively get your hands dirty with data analysis to validate feasibility. You define the work for the Applied Scientist (Optimization), the Machine Learning Engineer (the Builder), and the NLP Specialist (the Domain Expert). Your goal is to ensure we are solving the right problems with the right data, avoiding the trap of "AI looking for a use case."
What You Will Do
You will collaborate with Product Managers to translate abstract business goals (e.g., "reduce downtime") into concrete mathematical problems (e.g., "predict failure probability," "minimize scheduling cost," or "categorize maintenance logs automatically").
You will dive into large, complex industrial datasets to uncover hidden patterns, trends, and anomalies before any modeling begins.
For Optimization projects, you define the "Business Objective" (e.g., "balance cost vs. speed") that the Applied Scientist will encode into the mathematical solver. You ensure the math solves the actual business need.
You will design and execute experiments (A/B testing, hypothesis testing) to validate that our models are performing better than random chance. You prevent the team from being fooled by noise.
You will build the initial "Proof of Concept" models to test if a solution is possible. Once validated, you hand off the logic to the Engineers/Scientists for production hardening.
You define the "Definition of Success" (accuracy, precision/recall, ROI) that the rest of the technical team must aim for.
What We Expect From You
5+ years of experience in Data Science/Applied Statistics, comfortable working with messy, unstructured data.
Expert in Python and SQL (Pandas, Scikit-Learn, Statsmodels).
Strong statistical foundation (probability, hypothesis testing, causal inference) with sound judgment on when not to use deep learning.
Industrial/MRO exposure, including time-series, sensor data, or logistics.
Experience mentoring/leading a small technical team and collaborating across Operations Research, NLP (LLMs, embeddings, RAG), and Engineering, mindful of latency and cost constraints.

We are passionate experts who provide investment management and credit communities with innovative, user-friendly products which help their businesses grow in over 50 countries
Our environment is international, ego-free and relaxed. We believe in building confidence through giving clear responsibilities to our team members and managing to given goals and targets. We celebrate success and we enjoy each other’s company

We provide the investment management and credit communities with an innovative, user-friendly suite of products that, through detailed market and client insights, help their business to grow in over 50 countries.
We are transforming the financial services community which is in constant evolution.
With our agility, we make technology simple & accessible for our users. Let’s work together to achieve the best solutions for our clients.

Dans le cadre de sa croissance,
Linedata
, éditeur de référence dans le domaine de l’Asset Management et les crédits & Financement destinés aux
institutions financières
, recherche un
Data Scientist / ML Engineer
passionné par la donnée et l’intelligence artificielle.

title: Senior Generative AI (Gen AI) Engineer
description:
Senior Generative AI (GenAI) Engineer
Description
InnovationTeam
is a forward-thinking technology company that specializes in delivering advanced AI-driven solutions to enterprises and government organizations. We are currently seeking a highly skilled Senior Generative AI (GenAI) Engineer to join our AI team.
As a Senior GenAI Engineer at
InnovationTeam
, you will be responsible for designing, developing, and deploying large-scale generative AI solutions. You will work closely with cross-functional teams to translate business and technical requirements into robust AI systems and bring them into production.
This role requires deep expertise in modern AI architectures, large language models, and production-ready software engineering practices. The ideal candidate is highly motivated, detail-oriented, and capable of owning AI solutions end to end.
At
InnovationTeam
, we value innovation, collaboration, and continuous learning. We provide a flexible and inclusive work environment where your expertise is recognized and opportunities for professional growth are continuously supported.
Role Overview
We are seeking a skilled AI Engineer with a strong focus on Generative AI technologies to develop, optimize, and deploy AI Agents and solutions. The role involves working on real-world use cases and data analytics problems and delivering production-ready AI systems.
Key Responsibilities
Design, develop, and optimize Generative AI solutions (LLMs, RAG systems, AI agents)
Fine-tune, evaluate, and deploy large language models for enterprise use cases
Build and maintain end-to-end AI pipelines, from data ingestion to inference
Implement Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) using vector databases and knowledge stores
Integrate GenAI solutions with existing systems via APIs and microservices
Optimize model performance, latency, and cost on cloud platforms (OCI, AWS, Azure, or GCP)
Collaborate with product, data, and engineering teams to translate business needs into AI solutions
Ensure AI solutions follow best practices in security, governance, and responsible AI
Stay up to date with the latest developments in GenAI, LLMs, and AI tooling
Required Skills & Qualifications
Minimum 5 years of professional experience in AI / Machine Learning
Master's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, or a related field
Proficiency in programming languages such as python , Java , and Ruby
Strong experience with Large Language Models (LLMs) (e.g., GPT, LLaMA, Mistral, etc.)
Hands-on experience with Python and AI/ML frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow)
Solid understanding of NLP, deep learning, and transformer architectures
Experience with RAG architectures, vector databases (FAISS, OpenSearch, Pinecone, etc.)
Strong software engineering skills (APIs, REST, microservices, Docker, Kubernetes)
Experience deploying AI workloads on cloud platforms (OCI preferred)
Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills
Excellent command of English (written and spoken)
Nice to Have
Experience with GPU-based training and inference
Familiarity with MLOps / LLMOps practices
Experience in regulated or enterprise environments
Knowledge of AI governance, ethics, and compliance frameworks
What We Offer
Opportunity to work on cutting-edge Generative AI projects
Collaborative, innovation-driven environment
Competitive compensation package
Access to modern AI infrastructure and cloud resources
Professional growth and learning opportunities

description:
