title: Product and Quality Specialist
description:
Yassir
is the leading super App in the Maghreb region set to changing the way daily services are provided. It currently operates in
45 cities across Algeria, Morocco and Tunisia with recent expansions into France, Canada and Sub-Saharan Africa
. It is backed (:
$200M in funding
) by VCs from Silicon Valley, Europe and other parts of the world.
We offer on-demand services such as ride-hailing and last-mile delivery. Building on this infrastructure, we are now introducing financial services to help our users pay, save and borrow digitally.
Helping usher the continent into a digital economy era. We’re not just about serving people - we’re about creating a marketplace to bring people what they need while infusing social values.
About The Role
The role of a Product and Quality Specialist is pivotal in ensuring that the products manufactured and delivered meet the highest standards of quality and align with customer expectations. This multifaceted position combines elements of product development, quality assurance, and continuous improvement to guarantee excellence throughout the product lifecycle
As a recent graduate joining our team, you will have the opportunity to develop your skills in a dynamic and stimulating environment
TASKS
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to establish and execute robust quality control procedures. This involves defining quality checkpoints, conducting inspections, and implementing corrective measures to maintain product integrity
Work closely with product development teams to embed quality considerations at every stage of the product lifecycle. By integrating quality principles early in the design phase, potential issues can be identified and addressed proactively, ensuring that the final product meets quality standards
Perform various tests and audits throughout the manufacturing process to verify compliance with quality specifications. This includes conducting product inspections, analyzing samples, and utilizing testing equipment to assess product performance and reliability
Analyze quality data, including customer feedback and defect reports, to identify trends and areas for improvement. Utilize this information to implement corrective actions and drive continuous improvement initiatives aimed at enhancing product quality and customer satisfaction
Stay abreast of industry quality standards, regulations, and best practices. Ensure that all products meet regulatory requirements and adhere to relevant quality standards, certifications, and compliance frameworks
Requirements
Bachelor's or Master's degree in engineering, computer science, management, or a related field
Strong analytical skills and attention to detail
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively across teams
A proactive mindset with a commitment to continuous learning and professional development
Previous experience or coursework in quality assurance, product development, or manufacturing processes is advantageous
At Yassir, we believe in the power of diversity and the importance of an inclusive culture. So, if you're ready to bring your unique perspective and experiences to the table, then we're excited to listen.
Don't just apply for a job, come and be a part of our journey. Let's create a better tomorrow together.
We look forward to receiving your application!
Best of luck,
Your Yassir TA Team

Storyteller is a high growth B2B SaaS platform which allows companies to integrate Stories into their owned and operated platforms. Popularized by Instagram and Snapchat, Stories are perfectly suited for boosting user engagement, audience retention, and driving advertising revenue. For customers, building their own stories experience can take years and a lot of investment however Storyteller has solved this by allowing companies to integrate in days, not years all for one monthly cost.
Our end‑to‑end platform gives companies a best‑in‑class Stories experience in days with native iOS, Android, and Web SDKs, publishing tools, analytics, and ad support.
We work with many globally recognised clients, particularly within sport, so if you're a sporting fan this could be a great fit!
What You'll Make Happen
In AI Product Operations, you'll join a supportive, inventive team that values clarity, ownership, and getting things done. You’ll work remotely, collaborate with globally recognised sports organisations, and play a hands-on role in shaping how they use our product suite.
This role sits at the crossroads of project management, operations, AI-driven problem-solving, and product thinking. You don’t need years of product experience — just intelligence, curiosity, and the drive to figure things out fast.
Own day-to-day product workflows, decisions, and operational flow across our product suite.
Coordinate and deliver client projects -onboarding, configuration, feature adoption, and updates.
Use AI and internal tools to understand product behaviour, investigate issues, and generate solutions or improvements.
Apply AI to build and enhance scalable workflows, tools, and product solutions that accelerate delivery.
Maintain and improve internal processes, documentation, and delivery guides as we scale.
Work cross-functionally with product, engineering, and account teams to keep delivery smooth and unblock issues quickly.
What We're Looking For
Experience in product ops, SaaS ops, technical account management, or similar transferable skills with the ability to learn fast.
Strong understanding of sport and how sports organisations think about digital products.
An ability to think like a product owner - balancing user needs, technical constraints, and fast execution.
Genuine interest in technology and AI, with experience leveraging AI and effective prompting to accelerate decision-making, workflows, and problem-solving.
High ownership mindset - comfortable working across boundaries and figuring things out independently.
Excellent communication across technical and non-technical teams.
Ability to juggle multiple projects, requests, and client needs at speed.
Comfort working with data and structured information.
Reliable internet for remote work.
Nice to Have
Experience working directly with clients in a SaaS or B2B environment.
Experience in a fast-moving business (startup, scale-up, or agency).
Familiarity with content platforms, CMS tools, or sports-tech products.
RECRUITMENT PROCESS
First, you’ll be invited for a 15-minute chat, we’ll explain the role better and answer your questions.
We’ll then invite you to do a take home task (we’ll compensate you regardless of outcome) and then it’s just a final interview with the team.
And that’s it!

title: Data Analyst
description:
Job Description
Performs statistical modeling and analysis of structured and unstructured datasets to develop metrics, reports and visualizations of trends and patterns. Works with technology organization and business end-users to understand data and analysis needs and develop technical requirements. Partners with development and data management roles to identify and capture data required from internal and external sources. Creates and maintains statistical models for ongoing and ad hoc review and analysis of data. Uses data visualization programs, tools and techniques to generate dashboards, reports and presentations that aid in data storytelling, understanding and interpretation of trends and patterns of business importance. A seasoned, experienced professional with complete understating of principals, concepts, practices and standards. Works on problems of diverse scope where analysis of data requires evaluation of identifiable factors. Typically requires a degree and a minimum of 5 years of experience.

title: Chef de projet Marketing & Communication H/F
description:
À PROPOS DE EBHI :
EBHI
fait partie d’un groupe entrepreneurial multi-sectoriel en forte croissance, qui
développe, structure et accompagne des entreprises à fort potentiel, en France et à
l’international.
Au sein du groupe, le pôle Marketing & Communication interne fonctionne exactement
comme une agence de marketing & communication à 360°, au service des différentes
filiales, considérées comme des clients internes.
Le pôle regroupe l’ensemble des expertises habituellement présentes en agence :
stratégie marketing & communication
création de contenus (copywriting, design, social media)
coordination de projets
développement web et funnels
optimisation UX / CRO
data, tracking et pilotage de la performance
tests utilisateurs et amélioration continue
👉 Environnement multi-clients, multi-projets, multi-sectoriel, à rythme soutenu.

Le pôle Marketing & Communication est en phase de structuration avancée.
Les projets sont nombreux, variés et parfois complexes. Ils nécessitent :
une coordination rigoureuse,
une vision stratégique,
une orientation résultats,
une capacité à décider et arbitrer.
👉 Le poste de Chef de projet Marketing & Communication est un rôle clé et central, à
la fois :
pilote opérationnel,
interface client interne,
manager transversal,
relais stratégique.
FINALITÉ DU POSTE
Piloter les projets marketing & communication de bout en bout, garantir la
qualité des livrables, le respect des délais et des objectifs, tout en assurant un
rôle de coordination, de management transversal et de pilotage
stratégique orienté performance.
👉 Poste résolument orienté résultats.
PÉRIMÈTRE DU POSTE
Le Chef de projet est responsable :
de la coordination globale des projets,
de la relation avec les clients internes,
du pilotage des équipes projet pluridisciplinaires,
du suivi de la performance,
de la continuité opérationnelle du pôle.
Il/elle intervient à l’interface entre :
la Direction,
les équipes marketing, création, technique et data,
les filiales (clients internes),
les partenaires externes.
MISSIONS PRINCIPALES :
Pilotage & coordination de projets

Construire et suivre plannings, jalons et priorités
Garantir la livraison des livrables dans les délais
Identifier les risques et proposer des solutions
Arbitrer les priorités opérationnelles si nécessaire.
Relation client interne / Account management
Être l’interlocuteur principal des filiales
Comprendre les enjeux business et marketing de chaque projet
Reformuler les besoins et cadrer les attentes
Gérer validations, retours et ajustements
Garantir un haut niveau de satisfaction client interne.
Management & coordination d’équipe
Coordonner les équipes projet (création, technique, data)
Donner un cadre clair aux intervenants
S’assurer que chacun sait quoi faire, pour quand et pourquoi
Prendre le lead en l’absence de certains rôles clés
Contribuer à la structuration des méthodes et process.
Stratégie & performance
Participer à la réflexion stratégique des projets
Proposer des recommandations marketing & communication
Suivre les KPI (avancement, performance, impact)
Analyser les résultats et ajuster les actions
Être force de proposition orientée résultats.
Continuité opérationnelle (rôle renforcé)
Assurer temporairement :
une coordination renforcée,
un relais stratégique,
un pilotage global des projets
Garantir la continuité du pôle en cas de manque de ressources clés
PROFIL RECHERCHÉ :
Formation & parcours
Formation en :
gestion de projet
marketing
communication
management.
Expérience significative :
en agence,
ou en pôle marketing interne fonctionnant comme une agence,
ou en environnement multi-projets exigeant
👉 L’expérience réelle et la maturité priment sur le diplôme.
Compétences clés
Excellente organisation et priorisation
Forte autonomie
Vision globale des projets
Capacité à coordonner et faire avancer des équipes
Solide compréhension des enjeux marketing & communication
Capacité à décider et arbitrer
Très bonne compréhension des marchés français et internationaux, avec une
forte capacité d’adaptation culturelle.
Posture & Soft Skills
Leadership naturel
Communication claire et structurée
Fermeté sans autoritarisme
Orientation performance & résultats
À l’aise dans des environnements type agence
Capacité à gérer pression et imprévus.
LANGUES
Maîtrise parfaite du français (oral & écrit) obligatoire
Anglais professionnel indispensable
L’espagnol est un plus
📍Localisation : Tunis (Tunisie).
Contrat : Indépendant avec facturation mensuelle
Rémunération : à négocier lors de l'entretien avec indication dans les questions de
pré-sélection.
Disponibilité: immédiate
Temps de travail: temps plein ou partiel possible
COMMENT POSTULER ?
Pour
postuler
, assurez vous de répondre à toutes les questions de pré-sélection dans la section "Comment postuler" de l’annonce.
Les candidatures incomplètes (sans réponses aux questions de pré-sélection) ne seront pas prises en compte.

et participez à un projet digital & stimulant!

title: Customer Support Specialist, Tunis - Abacus
description:

We are looking for a skilled Customer Support Specialist to provide first and second level troubleshooting, incident resolution, and guidance on regulatory aspects of our solutions. This role requires expertise in regulatory and / or financial reporting, as well as strong analytical and problem-solving skills. The successful candidate will work closely with customers, product teams, and internal stakeholders to ensure timely issue resolution, knowledge sharing, and continuous improvement of support processes.

Business and Functional Troubleshooting
Provide expert support for our regulatory reporting products resolving client inquiries.
Analyze and resolve functional issues by reviewing business rules and configurations.
Investigate discrepancies in regulatory calculations and collaborate with research and development teams to find the best solution for customers.
Collaborate with technical teams to ensure functional issues are addressed effectively.
Customer Support Operations
Manage and resolve customer inquiries related to the functional use of the product, ensuring adherence to SLAs.
Guide customers on best practices for data mapping, report generation, and compliance validation.
Collaborate with engineering teams to escalate and resolve critical client issues.
Provide feedback to product teams on recurring functional issues and enhancement requests.
Why we should decide on you
Bachelor’s degree in finance, Business Administration, Economics, or a related field, or equivalent experience.
5+ years of experience in functional support, regulatory reporting, or financial software solutions.
Knowledgeable of regulatory frameworks (FinRep, CoRep, Basel, IFRS, Local Regulatory Reporting).
Experience working with financial reporting software and understanding business workflows.
Analytical skills to troubleshoot functional issues and work with cross-functional teams to resolve them.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English.
Experience in a global, multi-time-zone environment is a plus.
Key Skills & Attributes
Regulatory & Business Expertise: Good knowledge of financial regulations, compliance, and reporting standards.
Problem Solving & Analysis: Ability to diagnose and resolve business and functional issues effectively.
Customer Relationship Management: Strong ability to act as a trusted advisor for customers.
Time Management & Prioritization: Ability to manage multiple priorities and escalations effectively.
Collaboration & Communication: Ability to work cross-functionally with product, engineering, and other internal teams.
Learning Agility: Willingness to stay up to date with changing regulatory requirements.
Why you should decide on us
Let’s grow together, join a market leading SaaS company – our agile character and culture of innovation enables you to design our future.
We provide you with the opportunity to take on responsibility and participate in international projects.
In addition to our buddy-program, we offer numerous individual and wide-ranging training opportunities during which you can explore technical and functional areas.
Our internal mobility initiative encourages colleagues to transfer cross functionally to gain experience and promotes knowledge sharing.
We are proud of our positive working atmosphere characterized by a supportive team across different locations and countries and transparent communication across all levels.
Together we're better - meet your colleagues at our numerous team events.
To get a first impression, we only need your CV and look forward to meeting you in a (personal/virtual) interview!
Recognizing the benefits of working in diverse teams, we are committed to equal employment opportunities regardless of gender, age, nationality, ethnic or social origin, disability, and sexual identity.
Are you interest ? Apply now https://www.regnology.net/en/careers/
Role ID :
CS_2025_35
Apply for this job now
About Us
Regnology is a leading international provider of innovative regulatory, risk, and supervisory technology solutions (RegTech, RiskTech, and SupTech), of AEOI and tax reporting products, as well as of services along the Regulatory Value Chain for financial services. Regnology has been a partner for banks and regulators for 25 years. Until the end of 2020, the company was part of BearingPoint group and operated under the name BearingPoint RegTech. Since the sale of the RegTech business to private equity firm Nordic Capital, the company has been independent. In June 2021, the company joined forces with Vizor Software and recently changed its name to Regnology. In total, Regnology serves more than 7,000 financial services firms with reporting solutions. At the same time, the company enables more than 50 regulators and tax authorities on five continents to collect data from 34,000 firms in 60 countries. Regnology has a total workforce of over 770 employees at 17 office locations in 12 countries.

title: HR Administration Specialist
description:
About The Role
As part of our new-country launch in Tunisia, we are seeking an experienced HR Administration Specialist to establish and run core HR operations locally. This is a hands-on role that blends HR administration, employee relations, labor law expertise with the ability to operate autonomously, set up processes from the ground up, and ensure compliance with Tunisian labor law and Cognizant’s global standards
In This Role, You Will
Own the employee lifecycle in Tunisia: onboarding, contract issuance, addenda, probation tracking, offboarding.
Serve as the first point of contact for employee queries (contracts, benefits, leave, policies).
Maintain accurate personnel files, HRIS updates, leaves/absence, benefits and payroll inputs in line with Tunisian labor regulations.
Prepare HR letters and support disciplinary or grievance matters in coordination with Regional HR/Legal.
Work Model
At Cognizant, we strive to provide flexibility wherever possible, and we are here to support a healthy work-life balance though our various wellbeing programs. Based on this role’s business requirements, this is an onsite position requiring 5 days a week in the Cognizant office in Tunis.
The working arrangements for this role are accurate as of the date of posting. This may change based on the project you’re engaged in, as well as business and client requirements. Rest assured; we will always be clear about role expectations.
What You Need To Have To Be Considered
Bachelor’s/Master’s degree in Human Resources, Labor Law, Business Administration, or related field.
3–5+ years in a broad HR Generalist role with prior experience in a startup/new-country or scale-up context is a strong plus, including matrix relationships and work with HR shared services.
Solid knowledge of Tunisian employment law, HR administration, and payroll inputs.
Comfortable working autonomously and building processes from scratch.
Bilingual: Fluent French and English (written and spoken).
Key Competencies
Ownership & Execution: Able to prioritize, meet deadlines, and handle confidential matters with discretion.
Stakeholder Management: Strong partnering skills with business leaders and regional teams.
Communication: Clear, empathetic, and solutions-oriented; excellent writing skills for contracts and policy communication.
Problem-Solving: Pragmatic, data-driven, and proactive in removing operational blockers.
Cultural Agility: Comfortable navigating a global matrix while adapting to local norms.
These will help you stand out
Prior work in IT/Tech/Services environments and campus relations.

Job Description
Linedata est un éditeur de logiciels international ainsi qu’un fournisseur de services et de données à forte valeur ajoutée dédiés au secteur financier. Nous accompagnons nos clients — gestionnaires d’actifs traditionnels et alternatifs, administrateurs de fonds, établissements de crédit et de financement — dans leurs enjeux grâce à des solutions innovantes et performantes.
Forte de plus de 20 ans d’expérience dans la FinTech, Linedata offre à ses collaborateurs l’opportunité de développer une expertise solide à la fois en finance et en technologies, au sein d’un environnement international stimulant.
Nous humanisons la technologie:
Parce que nous sommes indépendants, fiables, accessibles et engagés ; parce que nos équipes sont unies, attentives, bienveillantes et passionnées ; et parce que nos experts sont de véritables partenaires spécialisés à l’écoute, nous concevons des solutions qui se veulent des références mondiales.
Portés par un environnement en pleine transformation et par des clients qui doivent s’adapter, motivés par des entreprises collaboratives partageant notre esprit entrepreneurial et nos ambitions, nous libérons leur potentiel créatif pour fluidifier les flux financiers et soutenir l’économie. Nous aidons chacun à développer ses compétences et à bâtir sa dignité pour les générations futures.
Votre mission en tant que QA:
Gérer les campagnes de tests (intégration, validation, non régression)
Concevoir les plans de tests
Exécuter les cas de test, manuellement ou via des outils d’exécution
Détecter et suivre les anomalies
Valider les corrections d’anomalies
Garantir la qualité des livrables
Compétences requises:
Solide capacité d’analyse et de résolution de problèmes
La connaissance du SQL est un atout
Profil recherché:
Titulaire d’un diplôme Bac+3 en informatique, data ou finance. Vous justifiez d’une première expérience en tant qu’analyste qualité dans un environnement ou un domaine similaire.
Job Requirement
Engagement pour la diversité :
Nous reconnaissons, célébrons et cherchons à accroître la diversité dans notre organisation. La diversité est un atout pour les organisations et est liée à une meilleure performance. Elle fait partie intégrante de notre façon de travailler et est impérative pour notre succès. En tant que Groupe international, nous pensons également que nos collaborateurs doivent refléter nos clients et les communautés locales.
Linedata est un employeur qui respecte l'égalité des chances. Nous nous engageons à respecter toutes les lois fédérales, étatiques et locales prévoyant l'égalité des chances en matière d'emploi, ainsi que toutes les autres lois et réglementations en la matière.
Nous nous engageons maintenir un environnement de travail exempt de harcèlement, de discrimination ou de représailles fondés sur la race, la couleur, la religion, la croyance religieuse, l'origine nationale, l'ascendance, la citoyenneté, le handicap physique ou mental, la condition médicale, l'information génétique, le statut marital, le sexe, le genre, l'âge, l'orientation sexuelle, le statut de vétéran et/ou de militaire, les congés médicaux protégés, le statut de victime de violence domestique, l'affiliation politique ou tout autre statut protégé par les lois fédérales, provinciales ou locales.

title: Cybersecurity Analyst
description:
Position Title:
Cybersecurity Analyst
Department:
IT Services
Reports To:
IT Department Senior Manager
Position Overview
The Cybersecurity Analyst is responsible for protecting SMU’s information systems, networks, and digital assets. This role plays a critical part in ensuring the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of institutional data while supporting SMU’s academic and operational activities.
The ideal candidate is a technically skilled professional with a strong sense of responsibility and ethics, capable of identifying risks, responding to incidents, and continuously improving SMU’s cybersecurity posture in line with international standards and best practices.
Key Responsibilities
Security Operations and Risk Management
Ensure the security of systems, networks, applications, and databases.
Identify, analyze, and remediate vulnerabilities across the IT environment.
Implement and maintain information security policies and procedures (ISMS / PSSI).
Monitor security events and manage incident response activities.
Auditing, Testing, and Compliance
Conduct security audits, vulnerability assessments, and penetration tests.
Support compliance with cybersecurity frameworks and regulations (ISO 27001, GDPR, etc.).
Maintain technical documentation, procedures, and security reports.
Monitoring and Tool Management
Operate and manage security tools such as SIEM, EDR, antivirus, and vulnerability scanners.
Perform log analysis, event correlation, and reporting using FortiAnalyzer.
Administer Fortinet solutions including FortiGate and FortiAnalyzer.
User Awareness and Continuous Improvement
Raise awareness among staff and students regarding cybersecurity best practices.
Conduct technology and regulatory monitoring to recommend improvements.
Contribute to strengthening SMU’s overall cybersecurity maturity.
Profile And Qualifications
Required Qualifications
Bachelor’s, Master’s or Engineering degree in Computer Science, Networking, Cybersecurity, or related field.
1–3 years of experience in cybersecurity or IT security (motivated junior profiles with strong internships will be considered).
Solid knowledge of network security (firewalls, VPN, IDS/IPS).
Experience with Linux and Windows Server environments.
Familiarity with application and database security.
Hands-on experience with Fortinet solutions (FortiGate, FortiAnalyzer).
Understanding of cryptography and identity/access management (IAM).
Basic knowledge of cloud security (Azure, AWS, or equivalent).
Fluent French and technical proficiency in English.
Preferred Qualifications
Experience with SIEM platforms, EDR solutions, and vulnerability scanners.
Exposure to cloud security architectures.
Professional certifications in cybersecurity (e.g., ISO 27001, Fortinet, CEH, Security+) are an asset.
Personal Attributes
Strong sense of confidentiality, integrity, and professional ethics.
Analytical mindset with strong problem-solving abilities.
Capacity to perform under pressure and manage crisis situations.
Team-oriented with good communication skills.
Autonomous, organized, and detail-oriented.
To apply, please send your CV and cover letter to hr.development@smu.tn

title: Information Security Officer
description:
Blauwtrust Groep provides high-quality financial services and products to the Dutch mortgage market. The company focuses on consumers as well as brokers and investors. Blauwtrust Groep consists of multiple operating companies, with more than eight hundred employees in total? that operate the entire value chain together, from management and administration to in-house advisory offices.
Vacancy: ISO Information
Security Officer
Blauwtrust Groep sets up group-wide strategy, goals and parameters in order to give guidelines to the operating companies and to provide them with the appropriate support in, amongst other things, information security.
In this context, we are looking to hire an Information Security Office (ISO) who will be based in Tunis and will support operating companies seated in the Netherlands and in Tunis. He or She reports to the Quality Assurance and Security Team Lead.
What are you going to do?
Contribute to the ongoing maintenance of ISO/IEC 27001 certification and support the effective operation of the Information Security Management System (ISMS).
Collaborate with business stakeholders to ensure timely and thorough security assessments are integrated into the lifecycle of new projects.
Contribute to the drafting and translation of information security policy into concrete guidelines.
Coordinate and implement information security activities within the ICT organization.
Analyzing and handling information security incidents.
Provide advice and support in the application of the technical information security policy.
Organizing risk analyses, ICT audits, disaster recovery tests and periodic security tests.
Who are we looking for?
Engineer (or master's degree) in information security related field with at least 4 years of experience as an ISO (experience in financial sector is a plus)
Extensive knowledge of certifications such as ISAE and ISO 27001 (ISO27001 certification is a plus)
Fluent in English, both verbally and in writing.
Competencies
Persuasiveness: Convincing others of ideas and points of view, even in the face of initial doubts.
Organizational sensitivity: Dealing effectively with organizational change and making use of informal networks.
Collaboration: Respect and integrate contributions from various disciplines.
Result orientation: Strategic management of results and policy plans with clear milestones.
Quality orientation: Identifies and takes action on quality improvements.
Discipline: Adheres to rules and procedures, even under great pressure
What do we offer you?
The Blauwtrust Groep is looking for top performers. Conversely, you can expect a lot from us. A pleasant, collegial culture with many development opportunities. You will receive a complete and attractive package of employment conditions.

title: SOC Analyst, Tunis - Remote
description:
Our Team
Cimpress is the world leader in mass customization. You’ve probably heard of our brands, including Vistaprint, WIRmachenDRUCK, Pixartprinting, and others. Our businesses can choose the solutions that work for them or assemble any custom combination they need. It makes it easier and faster to introduce new products, reach customers, and track orders. And this kind of innovation keeps customers coming back. Just last year, Cimpress generated $2.88B in revenue through customized print products, signage, apparel, packaging, and more.
We encourage our engineers to think like an owner – to continue to act small as we grow. Every team definests roadmaps and uses the programming languages and technologies that suit them best. This helps us have a significant impact at the enterprise level while still feeling small and nimble.
The Cimpress Security Team is a diverse team of technologists motivated to enable and empower our teams to move fast but securely. Our mission is to protect and secure Cimpress Business across the globe.
What You Will Do
Assess, triage, investigate and respond to alerts that originate from the cyber security product suite, e.g. SIEM, NGFWs, IDS/IPS, Anti-virus, Web Application Firewalls, EDR, etc.
Recognize potential, successful, and unsuccessful intrusion attempts and compromise then address them in accordance to established SOC procedures.
Carry out technical vulnerability assessments of IT systems to identifying potential vulnerabilities and make recommendations to control identified risks
Respond rapidly and effectively to IT security incidents, including performing forensics for evidence gathering and preservation.
Keep up to date with security trends, threats and control measures and apply them as needed throughout the organization.
Assist with the preparation of reports, brief nags, and documentation.
Contribute to the identify cation of process inefficiencies and continuous improvement of security monitoring, response, and controls.
Create content to expand the monitoring scope and optimize operations as needed (rules, automation, etc.)
Manage other activities that may arise through evolution and growth.
Your Qualifications
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or a related field.
1+ year experience in SOC operations and security event analysis or 3+ years of experience in IT security/network related field required.
Cyber security certifications preferred
Experience reviewing and correlating logs in a security capacity strongly preferred.
Understanding intrusion tools and techniques and detection methods at both the network and host level.
Knowledge of cloud security (AWS, Azure, GCP, etc.) required
Knowledge of threat intelligence tools and methodologies required
Experience with programming and scripting languages
Understanding of main compliance standards and data regulation requirements strongly preferred
Effective Communication both verbally and in writing.
Strong analytical skills, attention to detail and critical thinking
Approaching tasks with flexibility, proactivity and completing work to a high quality
Collaborative and team player.
Why You'll Love Working Here
We encourage our engineers to think like owners – to continue to act small as we grow. Every team defines their own roadmaps, and uses the programming languages and technologies that suit them best. This helps us have a big impact at the enterprise level while still feeling small and nimble. We have a Remote-First operating model and culture, with team members in over 15 countries and 30 US states, offering autonomy and the flexibility to work from home. Cimpress also provides collaboration spaces for team members to work physically together.
About Us
Led by founder and CEO Robert Keane, Cimpress invests in and helps build customer-focused, entrepreneurial mass customization businesses. Cimpress Technology develops cutting-edge, best-in-world software that our mass customization businesses use to create personalized physical (and digital) products, for over 17 million global customers. Our businesses can choose the solutions that work for them, or assemble any custom combination they need. This makes it easier and faster to do things like introduce new products, reach customers and track orders. And this kind of innovation keeps customers coming back.
Commitment to Diversity, Equity, & Inclusion
Cimpress exists to help our customers live their dreams. Each dream is unique – and the Cimpress team needs to be as well. We believe in the unique contributions of everyone within a diverse global organization. We are collaborative, inclusive, and innovative. We strive to role model and live an inclusive culture of fairness, respect and belonging for all. And we work together to empower each other, creating a space in which each of us can spark our next great idea.
Equal Opportunity Employer
Cimpress Technology, a Cimpress company, is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer. All qualified candidates will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, sex, national or ethnic origin, nationality, age, religion, citizenship, disability, medical condition, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender presentation, legal or preferred name, marital status, pregnancy, family structure, veteran status or any other basis protected by human rights laws or regulations. This list is not exhaustive and, in fact, in many cases, we strive to do more than the law requires.

title: Security Engineer - SecOps & Cloud
description:
Our Mission
Swapcard is the leading AI-powered event platform designed to drive revenue growth and foster meaningful connections at in-person and hybrid events. We recognize the importance of teamwork in successful events; that's why Swapcard is fueled by a team of innovators who are passionate about helping organizers build future-proof events.
Our Vision
At Swapcard, we believe in the power of meaningful connections. This belief fuels our commitment to pioneering modern solutions that empower organizers to create engaging event experiences.
Guided by our commitment to excellence and collaboration, we aim to redefine the landscape of event technology, setting new standards for engagement, accessibility, and impact.
Our Beliefs
At Swapcard, diversity is at the core of our success. With 42 nationalities represented among our 180+ team members, we champion diversity as a catalyst for creativity, collaboration, and unparalleled innovation.
We believe that by embracing a multitude of backgrounds, cultures, and viewpoints, we can truly understand and cater to the needs of our global community of event organizers and participants.
Our full remote opportunities empower our team to thrive, no matter where they are in the world, fostering a culture of flexibility and inclusion.
What you'll be doing?
Improve AWS security configurations (IAM, GuardDuty, CloudTrail, Amazon Inspector, etc.)
Manage and maintain security tools: EDR, MDM, DLP, compliance agents, etc
Coordinate with IT to ensure all laptops and endpoints follow security baselines
Review and respond to SOC provider alerts, investigate incidents, and manage the final remediation phase
Perform vulnerability management and coordinate patching with IT and DevOps
Improve and maintain WAF rules, anti-bot protections, and other application-layer defenses
Support access reviews, PCI-DSS reviews, and quarterly/monthly security tasks
Document and implement security configurations for cloud and SaaS tools
Contribute to Backup, DRP, and BCP testing in collaboration with Infra/IT teams
Provide input to security roadmap planning with practical improvements from operations
What you should have?
3+ years of experience in Security Engineering, SecOps, or Cloud Security
Hands-on experience with AWS security configuration and monitoring
Familiarity with EDR, MDM, DLP, and identity management (SSO, MFA)
Experience managing and troubleshooting security agents on endpoints
Knowledge of incident investigation and coordination with SOC services
Strong understanding of vulnerability management processes
Comfortable collaborating with IT, DevOps, and compliance teams
Bonus Points
Familiarity with Infrastructure-as-Code security (Terraform)
Knowledge of container security (Kubernetes, Helm)
Experience automating security tasks with scripts or CI/CD integrations
Exposure to SOC 2, PCI-DSS or ISO 27001 evidence collection and audits
Interest in continuous improvement and tool optimization
Swapcard’s Interview process
Our thorough interview process aims to identify exceptional talent to advance our mission while offering you a chance to explore your career potential at Swapcard.
Screening Interview with a recruiter from our people team
A remote exercise to demonstrate and assess your skills
Manager review with your future reporting manager
Leadership review with one of our department leaders
Reference check conducted by our people team
Offer
Swapcard's Values
🧠
Curious
:
We ask questions, try new things and take risks. We learn from one another and see mistakes as opportunities to grow—what matters most is how we react and learn from them. We are curious about what something is and why something is. Innovation thrives when curiosity drives.
🗝️
Value-Driven:
At Swapcard, we focus on making each decision count by prioritising outcomes that create meaningful value for our customers, team members, and partners.
💚
Human
:
At Swapcard, being human means fostering empathy, openness, and diversity to create a caring and collaborative community. We’re driven by a strong team spirit and a shared goal of building meaningful connections—both through our product and within our team.
✊
Resilient
:
We embrace challenges with optimism, creativity, and adaptability, constantly seeking innovative solutions and opportunities for growth.
👑
Ownership:
At Swapcard, we take responsibility and are accountable for our actions, driving success through initiative, trust, and accountability. True ownership means more than just completing tasks; it's about being proactive, investing ourselves fully in the outcomes, and fostering a culture of trust.
Benefits & Reasons To Join Swapcard
International team with 40+ nationalities (more on the way!) 🌍
Remote-first policy with headquarters in Paris 🗼
Thriving startup with career growth opportunities 🪴
Open-minded culture that appreciates differences 👽
Feedback-driven, supportive & curious team with a DIY mindset 🤔 🛠
Generous Paid Time Off to ensure you have time for what matters most ❤️🏡
Remote perks designed to optimize your working experience 🎁
In-person social gatherings to celebrate our achievements 🏝️
100% of your health insurance contribution paid by Swapcard 🏥
Work-from-home budget (one-off contribution for equipment in addition to your initial equipment setup) 🖇️
Co-working space budget to support remote work in professional environments 💼
Learning budget to help you develop new and existing skills 🤓
Mental health care initiatives to support your well-being 🧘
Equal Opportunity
Swapcard is committed to upholding equal employment practices and making merit-based employment decisions. We welcome individuals from all backgrounds, abilities, and experiences to apply, regardless of race, nationality, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, pregnancy status, age, marital status, and status as a veteran
We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Project Officer (Data & Research) (P)
description:
Job Description
Introduction
Established in 1951, IOM is a Related Organization of the United Nations, and as the leading UN agency in the field of migration, works closely with governmental, intergovernmental and non-governmental partners. IOM is dedicated to promoting humane and orderly migration for the benefit of all. It does so by providing services and advice to governments and migrants.
IOM is committed to ensuring a workplace where all employees can thrive professionally, while working towards harnessing the full potential of migration. Read more about IOM's workplace culture at IOM workplace culture | International Organization for Migration
Applications Are Welcome From First- And Second-tier Candidates, Particularly Qualified Female Candidates As Well As Applications From The Non-represented Member Countries Of IOM. For All IOM Vacancies, Applications From Qualified And Eligible First-tier Candidates Are Considered Before Those Of Qualified And Eligible Second-tier Candidates In The Selection Process. For The Purpose Of This Vacancy, The Following Are Considered First-tier Candidates
Internal candidates
Candidates from the following non-represented member states:
Antigua and Barbuda, Bahamas, Barbados, Comoros, Congo (the), Cook Islands, Dominica, Federated States of Micronesia, Grenada, Guinea-Bissau, Holy See, Iceland, Israel, Kiribati, Lao People's Democratic Republic, Madagascar, Marshall Islands, Namibia, Nauru, Palau, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Lucia, Samoa, Sao Tome and Principe, Seychelles, Solomon Islands, Suriname, Tonga, Tuvalu, Vanuatu.
Second Tier Candidates Include
All external candidates, except candidates from non-represented member states of IOM.
Context
Under the overall supervision of the Chief of Mission (CoM), the direct supervision of the Head of Governance and Data Unit and in close coordination with programmatic teams in IOM Tunisia, as well as Regional Data Hub (RTS: Data and Research) in Cairo and relevant Headquarters (HQ) departments, the Project Officer (Data and Research) will support the coordination and further development of data collection within the mission, produce data analysis and research outputs, and assist government counterparts in strengthening their capacities in data collection and analysis on migration issues. The data collection and analysis efforts will support the governance and policy work of the mission and inform evidence-based engagement with external stakeholders.
Responsibilities
Conduct geographic and thematic analyses and prepare inputs for reporting on issues relevant to the current migration dynamics in Tunisia.
Identify opportunities and contribute to the development of data collection methods and coordinate the data collection and analysis efforts of the mission.
Keep abreast of data and research developments at the regional and global levels and participate in coordination with other IOM missions to support a route-based approach in the analysis of migration trends.
Conduct data verification, triangulation and validation through multiple sources, both primary and secondary (including media monitoring, local authorities, network of key informants).
Support with literature/desk review and carry out extensive and in-depth data analysis of the datasets collected for the key reports, information materials, studies, and policy briefs.
Coordinate the implementation of data collection processes and overall data management activities, including data encoding, storage, and transfer, processing, visualization, and dissemination.
Ensure compliance with IOM data protection policies, data governance standard, and security standards, Information Technology and Communications (ITC) standards, and apply established best practices and lessons learned related to ethical data collection, use, sharing and dissemination.
Create an information system for the data collected, including offline and online platforms, application tools, and other systems.
Ensure the timely preparation and generation of mission-level information products, as well as information sharing and dissemination to all relevant channels.
Ensure data is relevant for policy making by coordinating data collection and analysis priorities with the policy work of the mission and the Global Compact for Migration (GCM) implementation processes.
Assist in the development of user-friendly training modules and workshop materials based on IOM’s Migration and the 2030 Agenda, GCM, expanding on the document’s guidance on monitoring indicators related to migration and the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and relevant initiatives in the region, including those involving the National Statistics Offices, other UN and other partners.
Support capacity-building activities on migration data collection, management, analysis and dissemination for IOM staff and external stakeholders, adapting existing materials to training objectives and audiences.
Provide technical support to national counterparts, including the National Institute of Statistics and the National Observatory on Migration, in analysing census and survey data related to migration and supporting additional surveys relevant to migration.
Provide inputs to evidence-based recommendations to improve data collection methods and internal workflows.
Perform such other duties as maybe assigned.
Qualifications
Education
Master’s degree in Migration Studies, Demography, Political or Social Science, Statistics, Data Science, Information Management, Economics, or related field from an accredited academic institution with two years of relevant professional experience; or,
University degree in the above fields with four years of relevant professional experience.
Accredited Universities are those listed in the UNESCO World Higher Education Database .
Experience
Experience in conducting research on migration and migration data, in particular with regard to migration in Africa and Europe, demonstrated by relevant professional assignments and/or contributions to analytical products;
Experience supporting capacity development activities and trainings, including online modalities;
Experience collaborating with national and international stakeholders; and,
Experience collaborating with National Statistics Offices.
Skills
Sound data collection, research, and report-writing skills are required;
Excellent writing skills in English are required;
High level of attention to detail is required;
Demonstrated statistical analysis skills are highly desirable; and
Ability to work with multiple data sources.
Languages
IOM's official languages are English, French and Spanish.
For this position, fluency in English and French is required (oral and written). Working knowledge of Arabic is an advantage.
Proficiency of language(s) required will be specifically evaluated during the selection process, which may include written and/or oral assessments.
Required Competencies
IOM's competency framework can be found at this link . Competencies will be assessed during the selection process.
Values
- all IOM staff members must abide by and demonstrate these five values:
Inclusion and respect for diversity: Respects and promotes individual and cultural differences. Encourages diversity and inclusion.
Integrity and transparency: Maintains high ethical standards and acts in a manner consistent with organizational principles/rules and standards of conduct.
Professionalism: Demonstrates ability to work in a composed, competent and committed manner and exercises careful judgment in meeting day-to-day challenges.
Courage: Demonstrates willingness to take a stand on issues of importance.
Empathy: Shows compassion for others, makes people feel safe, respected and fairly treated.
Core Competencies
– behavioural indicators Level 2
Teamwork: Develops and promotes effective collaboration within and across units to achieve shared goals and optimize results.
Delivering results: Produces and delivers quality results in a service-oriented and timely manner. Is action oriented and committed to achieving agreed outcomes.
Managing and sharing knowledge: Continuously seeks to learn, share knowledge and innovate.
Accountability: Takes ownership for achieving the Organization's priorities and assumes responsibility for own actions and delegated work.
Communication: Encourages and contributes to clear and open communication. Explains complex matters in an informative, inspiring and motivational way.
Managerial Competencies
- behavioural indicators – Level 2
Leadership: Provides a clear sense of direction, leads by example and demonstrates the ability to carry out the Organization’s vision. Assists others to realize and develop their leadership and professional potential.
Empowering others: Creates an enabling environment where staff can contribute their best and develop their potential.
Building Trust: Promotes shared values and creates an atmosphere of trust and honesty.
Strategic thinking and vision: Works strategically to realize the Organization’s goals and communicates a clear strategic direction.
Humility: Leads with humility and shows openness to acknowledging own shortcomings.
Notes
Internationally recruited professional staff are required to be mobile. Please refer to this link for guidance on IOM Job Category .
Any offer made to the candidate in relation to this vacancy notice is subject to funding confirmation.
This selection process may be used to staff similar positions in various duty stations. Recommended candidates will remain eligible to be appointed in a similar position for a period of 24 months.
The list of NMS countries above includes all IOM Member States which are non-represented in the Professional Category of staff members.
Appointment will be subject to certification that the candidate is medically fit for appointment, accreditation, any residency or visa requirements, security clearances.
Vacancies close at 23:59 local time Geneva, Switzerland on the respective closing date. No late applications will be accepted.
IOM has a zero-tolerance policy on conduct that is incompatible with the aims and objectives of the United Nations and IOM, including sexual exploitation and abuse, sexual harassment, abuse of authority and discrimination based on gender, nationality, age, race, sexual orientation, religious or ethnic background or disabilities
IOM does not charge a fee at any stage of its recruitment process (application, interview, processing, training or other fee). IOM does not request any information related to bank accounts.
IOM only accepts duly completed applications submitted through the IOM e-Recruitment system (for internal candidates link here ). The online tool also allows candidates to track the status of their application.
Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
For further information and other job postings, you are welcome to visit our website: IOM Careers and Job Vacancies

title: Operation Technologies & Compliance Specialist
description:
SIT S.p.a., à travers les deux divisions Chauffage et Smart Gas Metering, a créé des solutions intelligentes pour contrôler les conditions environnementales et mesurer la consommation pour un monde plus durable.
Multinational leader sur les marchés de référence et cotée sur le segment MTA de la Bourse italienne, SIT veut être le principal partenaire durable des solutions d'énergie et de climatisation au service des entreprises clientes, en accordant une grande attention à l'expérimentation et impact environnemental. Le Groupe est présent avec des sites de production en Italie, au Mexique, en Hollande, en Roumanie et en Chine, ainsi qu'une structure commerciale qui couvre tous les marchés mondiaux de référence. L'entreprise recherche la ressource suivante :
Operation Technologies & Compliance Specialist
Missions:
Ce poste consiste à gérer et maintenir l'infrastructure informatique de l'usine. Le technicien assurera le bon fonctionnement des systèmes informatiques de production (serveurs, MES, postes de travail) et l'intégration des applications logicielles et des automates programmables, contribuant ainsi à l'efficacité des processus de production, aux mises à niveau des lignes de production pour répondre aux nouvelles exigences des clients et à la sécurité du système informatique.
Gérer et maintenir la stabilité des serveurs et des systèmes informatiques.
Configurer et assurer le support des PC de production et de post-production.
Contrôler et superviser le système MES (Manufacturing Execution System).
Assurer le support et la gestion de l'intégration des applications PC et PLC pour la traçabilité et le contrôle des processus.
Élaborer les spécifications pour la mise en œuvre de nouvelles fonctions de contrôle et de traçabilité.
Mettre en œuvre une politique de sécurité informatique pour protéger le périmètre de production.
Appliquer les réglementations et les bonnes pratiques en matière de sécurité informatique (NIS2, ISO 27001, IEC 62443).
Résoudre les problèmes techniques et apporter un support aux utilisateurs internes.
Collaborer avec l'équipe d'automatisation et de maintenance pour garantir la continuité des activités.
Mettre en œuvre les procédures de sécurité informatique et de sauvegarde.
Profil recherché:
• Licence ou Master en informatique, génie informatique, automatisation industrielle ou cybersécurité.
• Certifications professionnelles :
o Auditeur principal ISO 27001 (sécurité de l’information).
o Certification IEC 62443 (sécurité des systèmes d’automatisation industrielle).
o Certifications en automates programmables et automatisation (par exemple, Siemens, Rockwell).
• Formations spécifiques sur la norme NIS2 et la conformité réglementaire pour les infrastructures critiques.
• Au moins 3 à 5 ans d’expérience dans un contexte industriel ou métallurgique.
• Gestion d’infrastructures informatiques complexes (serveurs, réseaux, systèmes MES et ERP).
• Projets d’intégration IT/OT (PC ↔ API, protocoles industriels tels que OPC UA et Modbus).
• Mise en œuvre de solutions de cybersécurité industrielle (segmentation du réseau, pare-feu industriels, IDS/IPS).
• Participation à des projets de numérisation d’usine (Industrie 4.0, IoT industriel).

title: Data Consultant
description:
Devoteam has been an international leader in consulting in digital strategy, technology platforms, cybersecurity and digital business transformation for 27 years.
With more than 11,000 employees around the world, we imagine and realize their ambitions thanks to the infinite possibilities given by digital platforms, shaping a new culture and way of working.
We are present in 25 countries, putting "Technology at the service of Man" in order to build a more sustainable world.
Devoteam Tunisie, a subsidiary of Devoteam Group, has been generating value for over 13 years. Working on both the African and international scales, our team consists of over 100 multidisciplinary consultants (consultants in digital transformation, developers, project managers, data and cyber security experts) working on enriching and innovative projects.
Job Description
We are seeking our next talents to work on data-related projects (at Strategy, Business, and Operations levels).
The ideal candidate will have a deep understanding of data analysis, management, and visualization, coupled with strong problem-solving and communication skills.
The Data Consultant will collaborate with clients and internal teams to assess data needs, develop strategies for effective data utilization, and implement solutions that drive business insights.
Responsibilities:
Analyze complex datasets to identify trends, patterns, and insights.
Interpret data to provide actionable recommendations for business improvement.
Work closely with clients to understand their business goals and data requirements.
Collaborate on the development of data strategies aligned with client objectives.
Design and implement data management processes to ensure data accuracy, completeness, and security.
Develop and maintain data documentation and metadata.
Create visually appealing and insightful reports and dashboards.
Communicate data findings effectively to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Identify and resolve data-related issues and challenges.
Propose innovative solutions to improve data processes and systems.
Stay updated on industry trends and advancements in data technologies.
Provide guidance on the selection and implementation of data tools and technologies.
Qualifications
Bachelor's or Master's degree in a relevant field (e.g., Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics).
Proven 2 years experience as a Data Consultant or in a similar role.
Technical:
Proficiency in data analysis tools and programming languages such as Python, R, SQL, and/or other relevant tools.
Strong understanding of data management, including data cleaning, preprocessing, and transformation.
Experience with data visualization tools like Tableau, Power BI, or other similar tools.
Problem Solving:
Strong analytical and critical thinking skills to analyze complex business problems and provide data-driven solutions.
Ability to identify patterns, trends, and insights from data to support strategic decision-making.
Communication:
Excellent verbal and written communication skills to effectively convey insights and recommendations to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Ability to communicate complex technical concepts in a clear and understandable manner.
Good communication skills in english and arabic.
Additional Information
This role necessitate mobility flexibility, allowing individuals to work interchangeably between Tunis and Riyadh (Saudi Arabia).

title: Senior Business Security Analyst M/F
description:
The ESI Business Security Office is a function of ADP’s Global Security Organization (GSO). They enable the ADP Employer Services International (ESI) organization to effectively manage security within the business..
The Senior Business Security Analyst reports into the EMEA Senior Director Business Security Office and he/she represents the GSO and Business Security Office interfacing with the respective Romania and Tunisia business units and stake-holders to consult on, drive and support:
Business Resilience
Risk Management
Incident Management
Relationship management
Security Culture
Technology Transformation
Product Security
Scope of Responsibilities
:
Deliver dedicated security services for the Romania and Tunisia.
Act as a Security Consultant to the BU Senior Leaders, management and associates, to support them with security programs and resources.
Help mitigate risk, protect privacy, and meet unexpected security challenges, anticipate emerging threats.
Represent GSO’s converged security organization; coverings a wide range of disciplines including Cyber, Physical Security, and Fraud.
Operational focus areas:
Ambassador and evangelist for GSO’s Security Program. Provide Leadership in execution of the global security program at the BU level. The Business Security Analyst role includes but is not limited to:
Assess and monitor risks associated with all processes and technologies within the BU, including application and product vulnerabilities. Ensure governance and support with related remediation process to closure.
Support ADP’s End User Computing, Global Enterprise Technology Services functions in roll-out of all ADP security technologies within the region - such as DLP, End point protection, NAC, MFA, etc.
Provide technical consulting around customization of security technological solutions and other security programs (Business Resilience, Zero-Trust, etc.) to make them relevant and effective regionally.
Provide risk and other relevant metrics to the BUs as necessary or assigned. Perform other related duties as assigned.
Initiate and support Security training and awareness and communications programs, customized to maximize reach and impact locally/regionally.
Monitor and support the Incident Management process in alignment with the applicable structure of local Business Security Champions for the scope/region. Consult and contribute to incident reduction programs in direct liaison with BU Business Process Improvement teams and stakeholders.
Understand the ADP GSO Security program (security policies, guidelines, best practices, checklists, etc.).
Provide coordination, operational service assurance and reporting of various activities such as threat and vulnerability management (incl. alerting services, application security, and investigations, etc.).
Support and act as a local extension of GSO’s Security Advocacy, Communications and Training functions. Engage across the organization to discuss client security requirements and explain the Security Advocacy program. Provide consult with prospect and client inquiries, audit requests and the review of security terms in contracts by engaging directly with client service, sales, and legal teams.
Knowledge and Skill Requirements:
Bachelor's degree in Risk Management, Information Security, Auditing, or Compliance.
English language proficiency required and
French
languages definitely a plus.
Information Security related certifications are desirable.
3+ years security and/or risk management experience in a large and complex business environment.
Experience in business process improvement.
Proficiency with MS Office 365 applications.
Knowledge of security methodologies, policies, (industry)standards, certifications and best practices.
Knowledge of Information systems and operations.
Ability to clearly explain technical concepts using non-technical language.
Basic knowledge of HR and payroll processes is advantageous.

title: Data Governance Consultant
description:
Devoteam is a leading consulting firm focused on digital strategy, tech platforms and cybersecurity. By combining creativity, tech and data insights, we empower our customers to transform their business and unlock the future.
With 30 years’ experience and more than 12,000 employees across Europe, the Middle East and Africa, Devoteam promotes responsible tech for people and works to create better change.
Our focus is to help our clients' win the digital battle, adapted to our clients’ business challenges, with a unique consideration to its impact on their systems and structures.
Job Description
Design and implement the data governance framework, operating model, and roles and responsibilities.
Develop and implement data governance process workflows.
Conduct requirement gathering and business analysis related to data governance initiatives.
Support data architecture activities in coordination with data governance objectives.
Participate in data maturity assessment sessions, gap analysis, and alignment with Enterprise Architecture teams and data strategy.
Coordinate with project teams to ensure that data standards and models align with enterprise architecture and applicable standards.
Define data governance–related roles and responsibilities, processes, and procedures.
Establish a common understanding and visibility of all organizational data assets.
Build strong working relationships with business stakeholders and provide business-impact-driven analysis, communicating results effectively.
Qualifications
Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Business Administration, or a related field.
2–4 years of experience in data governance, data management, or related domains.
Certifications such as DAMA, Informatica, or Collibra are highly preferred.
Strong understanding of data governance principles, data quality management, and enterprise data standards.
Excellent communication, analytical, and stakeholder management skills.
Ability to work collaboratively in a cross-functional environment.
Additional Information
Opportunity to contribute to a strategic and high-impact data initiative.
Collaborative and innovative work environment.
Professional growth and certification opportunities.

title: AI Video Marketing Internship
description:
About M365Consult
M365Consult is a fast-growing European Microsoft 365 consultancy specializing in digital transformation, cybersecurity, and AI-driven solutions for clients across the DACH region (Germany, Austria, Switzerland). Our team is passionate about innovation, collaboration, and building a strong brand presence across digital platforms. We are looking for motivated individuals eager to learn, grow, and help us communicate complex Microsoft topics through engaging video content.
To expand our brand and accelerate our reach, we are launching a remote internship for a motivated AI Video Marketing Intern, someone eager to learn how to create viral video content that speaks to both Microsoft experts and the organizations working with M365Consult on their cloud and security journey.
What This Internship Is About
This is not just about making videos; it is about building scalable, viral content systems. You will learn how to plan, generate, and scale video content using cutting-edge AI tools, collaborate with consultants and automation engineers, and simulate processes that will power the video content engine of a Microsoft-focused consulting brand.
You will gain hands-on experience with leading AI video tools and be part of shaping M365Consults' visual voice across:
YouTube Shorts and Instagram Reels
TikTok-style viral content
LinkedIn video posts and carousels
Client testimonial and success-story videos
Product and solution demo videos (Microsoft 365, security, Azure, AI)
Educational explainer content for business and IT audiences
AI-generated visual sequences and animations
Podcast- and webinar-to-video content repurposing
And most importantly, you will be challenged to navigate independently, grow fast, and own your learning path.
Structure & Expectations
Duration: 36 months (performance-based)
Format: Self-paced internship (flexible hours, fully remote)
Onboarding: Access to our AI tools, resources, and team for initial orientation
After onboarding: You are expected to drive your own progress and propose new viral video ideas aligned with our Microsoft 365 and security topics
Support: Light supervision, regular check-ins, and access to mentors, but creative initiative is key
What You'll Get Hands-On With
Sora, Veo3, HeyGen, Synthesia, Runway ML, Midjourney, and other cutting-edge AI tools for video and visuals
Suno (or similar) for AI audio generation and background music creation
AI prompt engineering for video, image, and audio content
Real-world viral video workflows: from manual creation to automation-ready processes
Cross-functional collaboration with consultants, tech teams, and social media/marketing managers
A platform to build a portfolio of viral content across multiple platforms and formats, focused on Microsoft 365, security, and AI consulting topics
Who We Are Looking For
You do not need to be an AI video expert (yet). We are looking for:
Strong interest in video content creation, social media trends, or tech/business topics
Curious, self-motivated learners who love experimenting with new AI tools
Understanding of what makes content perform on TikTok, Instagram, YouTube, and LinkedIn
Passion for Microsoft technologies, IT consulting, cybersecurity, or digital marketing
Comfortable working independently and building your own creative structure
Excellent written English and basic German (preferred)
Bonus: Experience with video editing, Canva, TikTok, Instagram, or any AI video tools
Why This Internship Matters
This is a real opportunity to transition into a full-time role in an AI-driven, remote-first consulting company that is innovating in the Microsoft 365 and security space. If you can demonstrate viral content creation skills, organize video production pipelines, and show that you learn fast, you can grow into a permanent member of the M365Consult team.
Learn fast. Create viral. Build with us.
This is your chance to create the future of consulting video content.
#Internship #AIVideoMarketing #ViralContent #MicrosoftConsulting #RemoteInternship #M365Consult #VideoMarketing #TikTokCreator #YouTubeShorts #AIVideoTools #DACHRegion #DigitalTransformation

SentinelOne
Scripting PowerShell
Windows 11, 10
MacOS

title: Cryptography Engineer
description:
We are seeking a Cryptanalyst to strengthen our cryptography and data protection framework across critical systems.
The role ensures encryption standards, key management, and cryptographic protocols align with DORA operational resilience requirements, supporting secure digital services, cloud deployments, and regulated operations.
Key Responsibilities
Cryptanalysis & Algorithm Evaluation
:
-Conduct applied cryptanalysis on encryption algorithms, hashing functions, and key-exchange mechanisms.
-Identify implementation weaknesses such as weak key management or insecure cipher modes.
Implementation Review & Secure Engineering
-Analyse application code and infrastructure configurations for cryptographic misuses.
-Validate the correctness of API integrations, certificate handling, and secrets management.
Automation & Tooling
-Develop scripts and tooling in Python, Bash, or PowerShell to automate analysis tasks.
Risk Management & Compliance Support
-Contribute to cryptographic risk assessments and prioritization processes.
-Support compliance with NIST encryption standards, ISO/IEC 27001 controls, and internal cryptography policies.
Required Skills
Deep understanding of modern symmetric and asymmetric cryptography (AES, RSA, ECC).
Hands-on experience with cryptanalysis tools and techniques.
Strong scripting and automation skills (Python, PowerShell, or Bash) to assess or simulate cryptographic attacks.
Familiarity with secure key management, PKI, and HSMs.
Knowledge of cloud security and DevSecOps practices for cryptographic integration.
Strong knowledge of regulatory and risk frameworks: DORA, ISO 27001, NIST, GDPR.
Experience & Profile
2–5 years of experience in cybersecurity, cryptography, or security engineering.
Hands-on exposure to cryptographic libraries or secure protocol analysis.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Experience in regulatory or audit-driven environments.

title: 03-C Production Inspector Production Facility
description:
LEONI is a global leading provider of products, solutions and services for energy and data management in the automotive industry. With around 86,000 employees in 21 countries and consolidated sales of EUR 3.9 billion in 2025 (excluding the former Automotive Cable Solutions division), we are a leading supplier of complex wiring systems and customer‑specific cable harnesses.
Since 2025, we have been part of the Luxshare Group, a global technology company headquartered in China. Together, we are shaping the future of mobility. Join us and help drive the transformation of the automotive world.
Job Description
Accomplishes specific tasks within the Assembly area, (e.g. representative not full list)
Performs all checks as defined in the Quality control plan and records the required data
Identifies non-conforming products with "red card" process
Distributes and collects PDEK or other control charts
Gives feedback to line and to WPA for all non-conformities found on the assembly line (e.g. electrical test, clip test, inspection points, care points, etc.) by using the stop at first defect process
Inspects 5S / housekeeping application within the area of responsibility
Identifies areas of product risk e.g. material on the floor, terminals damaged, bent within holders, stands, mixed components, etc.
Checks that operators are qualified to work on the station in line with the skills matrix
Checks adherence to OK to Start procedure at the start of each shift
Performs check of error proofing conformity within the assembly area (e.g. good/bad sample test release, poka- yoke, jigs, etc.)
Varifies that assembly aid tools are available and used in proper condition (e.g. convolute and corrugated tube aids, pvc tube aids, etc.)
Validates the completion of the packing process and conformity according to work instructions
Ensures that in-line and off-line reworks are performed by qualified operators with released tools and respecting rework procedures
Inspects if documentation in workstation according to control plan and internal standards is followed
Audits final harness inspection in accordance with the QM plan and or additional care points
Qualifications
level of Education: University Degree
Branch Of Study: all branchs
Experience: 1 year of experience
Social competences: Communication /Social & intercultural sensitivity / Change management /Target & resultorientation/ Motivation & resilience /Self-organization /Conflict management / Teamwork
Professional competences: Adult Learning Theory/Blended Learning/ELearning Concepts/Skills and Competency Modeling/Training Management /Leadership Development Programs
