title: Automation Tester
description:
What you'll do
As a Automation Tester at Regnology, you will play a key role in ensuring the quality and reliability of our cloud-ready products. You will design, implement, and maintain automated test solutions that help us deliver robust, high-performing software. Your technical expertise, proactive learning attitude, and strong communication skills will be essential to your success.
Key Responsibilities
Design and implement automated test suites for complex, distributed, cloud-based applications.
Build and maintain test automation frameworks using industry-standard tools and best practices.
Collaborate closely with software engineers, architects, and product teams to integrate testing seamlessly into the development process.
Provide technical leadership and mentorship to other team members.
Continuously refine and improve testing strategies, tools, and methodologies to increase efficiency and reliability.
Stay current with emerging technologies, testing frameworks, and industry trends to drive innovation in test automation.
Why we should decide on you
Qualifications
3+ years of hands-on experience in test automation for web applications.
Solid understanding of testing concepts including functional, performance, and security testing.
Strong problem-solving skills and ability to learn through experimentation (“learn by doing”).
Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with experience working closely with developers and product teams.
Must-Have Skills
Proven experience with modern test automation frameworks (especially Cypress and Playwright) using JavaScript/TypeScript.
Experience integrating automated tests into CI/CD pipelines (e.g., Azure DevOps, Jenkins).
Knowledge of REST API testing and related tooling (e.g. Postman)
Proficiency in using source code version control systems (e.g., Git)
Strong analytical mindset and focus on reliability and maintainability of test suites.
Nice-to-Have Skills
Good understanding of containerized environments — e.g. Docker, Kubernetes, Helm, OCI images, and general cloud deployment workflows.
Familiarity with relational databases such as PostgreSQL or SQLite, and ability to validate data-driven functionality.
Experience with cloud platforms (e.g., Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud Platform).
Familiarity with performance testing (e.g. jMeter or k6)
Why you should decide on us
Let’s grow together – our start-up character enables you to design our future
We promote remote working and flexible working hours to create a positive work-life balance
We provide you with the opportunity to take on responsibility and participate in international projects
We offer you a stable job that is not affected by crises
We offer numerous individual and wide-ranging training opportunities, in addition to our buddy-program
We are proud of our positive working atmosphere characterized by a supportive team across different locations and countries and transparent communication across all levels
Together we're better - meet your colleagues at our numerous team events
We care about body and soul, we offer you numerous benefits such as meal vouchers & free drinks in the office.
To get a first impression, we only need your CV
in English
and look forward to meeting you in a (virtual) interview!
Even if you do not live in the Tunis area, we are looking forward to your application. Thanks to flexible working hours and remote working, we will find a solution that suits you.
Recognizing the benefits of working in diverse teams, we are committed to equal employment opportunities regardless of gender, age, nationality, ethnic or social origin, disability, and sexual identity.
Are you interested? Apply now!
https://www.regnology.net
R&D 2026 01
Apply for this job now
About Us
Regnology is a leading international provider of innovative regulatory, risk, and supervisory technology solutions (RegTech, RiskTech, and SupTech), of AEOI and tax reporting products, as well as of services along the Regulatory Value Chain for financial services. Regnology has been a partner for banks and regulators for 25 years. Until the end of 2020, the company was part of BearingPoint group and operated under the name BearingPoint RegTech. Since the sale of the RegTech business to private equity firm Nordic Capital, the company has been independent. In June 2021, the company joined forces with Vizor Software and recently changed its name to Regnology. In total, Regnology serves more than 7,000 financial services firms with reporting solutions. At the same time, the company enables more than 50 regulators and tax authorities on five continents to collect data from 34,000 firms in 60 countries. Regnology has a total workforce of over 770 employees at 17 office locations in 12 countries.
Any questions? Feel free to get in touch:
recruiting@regnology.net

title: Privileged Access Management Engineer
description:
We are seeking a skilled Privileged Access Management (PAM) Engineer to design, implement, and maintain our PAM solution. PAM Engineer needs to have a deep understanding of privileged access security principles and experience working with industry-leading PAM tools. This role will involve integrating PAM systems into our IT infrastructure, automating processes, and ensuring the security and compliance of privileged accounts across the organization.
Key Responsibilities:
PAM Implementation & Maintenance:
Design, deploy, and configure PAM solutions such as Wallix, CyberArk, BeyondTrust.
Integrate PAM systems with existing IT infrastructure, including Active Directory, LDAP, and cloud environments.
Ensure the scalability, availability, and performance of PAM systems.
Privileged Account Management:
Automate privileged account provisioning, rotation, and de-provisioning processes.
Manage and monitor privileged accounts, credentials, and access permissions across all systems.
Security & Compliance:
Enforce privileged access policies and ensure compliance with regulatory standards.
Perform regular audits of privileged account activities and provide detailed reports.
Monitor PAM logs for anomalies, unauthorized access, or potential security incidents.
Required Skills:
Expertise in deploying and managing PAM tools.
Strong understanding of authentication protocols (e.g., Kerberos, SAML, OAuth) and directory services (e.g., Active Directory, LDAP) and Public Key Infrastructure
Hands-on experience with OS:
-Windows Server: RDS,NPS,Radius
-Linux: Basic commands
Scripting and automation skills (e.g., PowerShell or Bash)
Knowledge of cybersecurity principles, including least privilege, zero trust, and privileged access controls.
Required Profile:
Formal Education: master’s degree in computer science, Cybersecurity, or related field.
● Professional Experience: Minimum of 2 to 5 years in a similar role.
● Communication: Effective communication skills, both oral and written.
● Analytical Ability: Exceptional problem-solving and analytical skills.
● Teamwork: Ability to collaborate effectively within a team.
● Language Proficiency: Fluent in technical English.

title: Ingénieur Identity and Access Management
description:
Description De L'entreprise
Sopra HR Software, filiale de Sopra Steria, offre des solutions RH complètes parfaitement adaptées aux besoins des Directions des Ressources Humaines et aux organisations de moyennes et grandes tailles, des secteurs public et privé.
Spécialiste du pilotage des RH, de la paie et du talent management dans un contexte local et international, Sopra HR accompagne plus de 950 clients, en mode « on-premise » ou mode cloud.
Partenaire de la réussite de la transformation digitale de ses clients vers la RH 3.0, Sopra HR, acteur global des Ressources Humaines, privilégie la Co-innovation, favorise les enjeux de performance RH et met en avant l’expérience collaborateur.
Description Du Poste
Vous intégrez la Direction des Systèmes d’Information qui a pour rôle de garantir le bon fonctionnement des technologies de l’information au sein du Groupe Sopra Steria.
Afin de répondre aux enjeux et à la stratégie numérique globale du Groupe, la DSI gère la conception, la mise en œuvre, et le déploiement de solutions informatiques fiables et adaptées aux besoins. Elle assure la gestion et l’optimisation des infrastructures technologiques ainsi que son maintien en condition opérationnelle et de sécurité.
Au sein de la DSI, vous intégrez l’équipe de gestion des identités (IAM) composée de 10 personnes présentes en France et à l’international.
Votre rôle et vos missions
:
Vous contribuez au maintien et à l’évolution des solutions de gestion des identités du groupe basées sur les solutions de Microsoft Entra ID et Active Directory.
Dans ce cadre vous effectuez :
L’administration et l’exploitation opérationnelle de la plateforme Microsoft Entra ID et Active Directory.
L’administration et l’amélioration continue de la sécurité des solutions de gestion d’identités dans le Cloud et On Premise
La gestion et l’industrialisation des d'outils d’administration et de provisioning des identités.
L’administration et l’évolution de la solution de gestion de compte à privilège
Vous participez à la définition et à l’évolution des architectures mise en œuvre au sein du groupe Sopra Steria.
Vous travaillez en relation avec les équipes de la Cybersécurité de Sopra Steria pour contrôler et maintenir la sécurité des annuaires.
Environnement technique:
Microsoft365, Active Directory, Azure AD, EntraID, ADFS, AD Connect, Powershell, PKI (Public Key Infrastructure), Microsoft identity Manager.
Qualifications
Vous êtes diplômé d’une école d’ingénieur Bac+5 ou formation universitaire équivalente. Vous disposez d’une expérience de 8 ans et plus dans l’implémentation des solutions de gestion des identités de Microsoft
Vous maitrisez les solutions de gestion des identités de Microsoft, de scripting powershell, de l’infrastructure Active Directory et Azure AD.
Maîtrise du français et de l’anglais professionnel.
Excellentes aptitudes en communication, leadership et coordination.

title: Talent Aquisition Partner
description:
Job Description
As a
Talent Acquisition Partner
, you will play a critical role in driving full-cycle recruitment across our global business units. You’ll collaborate with senior stakeholders, lead strategic hiring initiatives, and ensure an outstanding candidate and hiring manager experience. You will also leverage your
Workday ATS
expertise to manage requisitions, optimize workflows, and ensure data accuracy and compliance.
Job Description: Talent Acquisition Partner
Role Overview
As a
Talent Acquisition Partner
, you will play a pivotal role in shaping the workforce of our global business units. This position goes beyond filling vacancies — it is about becoming a trusted advisor to leaders, driving recruitment strategies that align with business objectives, and ensuring that every candidate touchpoint reflects our commitment to excellence. You will operate in a fast-paced, dynamic environment where innovation, collaboration, and stakeholder partnership are at the heart of success.
Key Responsibilities
Full-Cycle Recruitment Leadership
Manage end-to-end recruitment processes across multiple business functions and regions.
Partner with hiring managers to define role requirements, craft compelling job descriptions, and set clear expectations for the recruitment process.
Conduct proactive sourcing campaigns using multiple channels, including LinkedIn Recruiter, talent databases, referrals, and market mapping.
Strategic Talent Partnering
Act as a consultant to senior stakeholders, providing market insights, competitive intelligence, and recommendations on talent acquisition strategies.
Support workforce planning and succession initiatives by aligning hiring strategies with long-term business needs.
Drive employer branding initiatives to attract diverse, top-tier talent globally.
Candidate Experience Excellence
Ensure every candidate receives a positive, transparent, and engaging experience from application to onboarding.
Conduct structured interviews, facilitate assessments, and manage offer negotiations.
Act as an ambassador of our values and culture, building meaningful connections with prospective employees.
Hiring Manager Engagement
Guide managers through hiring best practices, interview techniques, and inclusive recruitment approaches.
Provide regular updates on recruitment progress, pipeline health, and time-to-fill metrics.
Influence decision-making by presenting data-driven insights and recommendations.
ATS & Process Optimization (Workday)
Leverage your expertise in Workday ATS to manage requisitions, track candidate pipelines, and maintain accurate recruitment data.
Continuously evaluate and optimize recruitment workflows to ensure efficiency and compliance.
Ensure full compliance with global data protection and employment regulations.
Qualifications & Experience
Proven experience in full-cycle recruitment, ideally within a global or matrix organization.
Strong track record of partnering with senior business leaders and influencing strategic hiring decisions.
Deep knowledge of sourcing strategies, market mapping, and talent engagement techniques.
Hands-on experience with Workday ATS (or equivalent systems) and the ability to optimize system usage.
Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills.
Ability to balance multiple priorities, meet deadlines, and adapt to changing business needs.
Passion for delivering an outstanding candidate and hiring manager experience.
What We Offer
Opportunity to partner with global leaders and influence business-critical hiring decisions.
A dynamic, inclusive, and innovation-driven culture.
Tools, resources, and training to grow your career in talent acquisition.
Exposure to global markets, diverse industries, and cutting-edge recruitment practices.
At DXC Technology, we believe strong connections and community are key to our success. Our work model prioritizes in-person collaboration while offering flexibility to support wellbeing, productivity, individual work styles, and life circumstances. We’re committed to fostering an inclusive environment where everyone can thrive.
Recruitment fraud is a scheme in which fictitious job opportunities are offered to job seekers typically through online services, such as false websites, or through unsolicited emails claiming to be from the company. These emails may request recipients to provide personal information or to make payments as part of their illegitimate recruiting process. DXC does not make offers of employment via social media networks and DXC never asks for any money or payments from applicants at any point in the recruitment process, nor ask a job seeker to purchase IT or other equipment on our behalf. More information on employment scams is available
here
.

title: Operations Associate (Movements)
description:
Job Description
Since the inception of IOM in 1951, Movement Operations have been and continue to be a fundamental pillar of the Organization’s work. The organized movement of persons in need of international migration assistance is a primary mandate of the Organization and a cornerstone of IOM’s operations. Movement Operations Units in various IOM Country Offices, coordinated under the Resettlement and Movement Management (RMM) Unit in the Division of Movement, Resettlement and Labour Pathways (MRLP) at IOM’s Headquarters, are responsible for all aspects of travel for migrants and refugees under IOM’s auspices, in accordance with the various framework agreements with resettlement and receiving Governments and partners across the spectrum of the Organization's programmes.
Responsibilities
Under the general supervision of the Senior Protection Coordinator and direct supervision of the Movement Operations Officer ,the Movement Operations Associate is responsible for undertaking movement operations activities, with the following duties and responsibilities:
Coordinate, schedule and book travel for individuals upon receipt of travel-ready status and/or request in accordance with travel requirements, including but not limited to the distribution of Advance Booking Notifications (ABNs), updates, domestic flights, cancellations and departure notifications.
Organize and complete all bookings in a timely manner and in accordance with the Handbook of IOM Tariffs (HIT) and standard operating procedures (SOPs) from the Division of Resettlement and Movement Management (RMM). Under the supervision of the direct supervisor, distribute travel information to internal and external stakeholders.
Compile and analyze descriptive statistics, using I-GATOR to capture costs and prepare travel loan paperwork as specified in SOPs and in accordance with host government’s procedures.
Create movement data files, by ABN, for all individuals in accordance with SOPs and for IOM accountability.
In accordance with local practices and RMM guidelines and standards, and in close coordination with supervisors, identify and assign escorts to accompany vulnerable individuals.
In coordination with the supervisor, process exit permission paperwork and travel documents in accordance with established standards and the local guidelines.
Conduct identity and document verification prior to the distribution of travel documentation to refugees, immigrants and migrants.
Conduct pre-departure counselling on pre-embarkation procedures and special needs during travel (such as meals, medication, wheelchairs and medical conditions) as needed. Identify beneficiary vulnerabilities and coordinate appropriate action to ensure they are addressed.
Provide regular feedback on work being accomplished to the supervisor and keep the supervisor immediately informed of any issues that arise.
Undertake duty travel as needed to participate in meetings, trainings and other programme activities, including in their planning and facilitation.
Undertake duty travel as required and delegated to participate in meetings, trainings and other programme activities.
Demonstrate an in-depth understanding of applicable SOPs, related systems and databases, and IOM Standards of Conduct. Maintain and ensure the confidentiality and integrity of all relevant paperwork in line with SOPs, Standards of Conduct and relevant data protection rules.
Uphold RMM Protection Mainstreaming Minimum Standards while carrying out all duties and responsibilities and demonstrate the ability to remain professional, impartial and unbiased during all interactions with migrants and colleagues per the IOM Standards of Conduct and instruction on the prevention of sexual exploitation and abuse (PSEA). Alert the supervisor or management of any non-compliance to SOPs, RMM Protection Mainstreaming Minimum Standards or IOM Standards of Conduct by IOM personnel or partners.\
Perform other related duties as assigned.
Qualifications
EDUCATION
High school diploma with 5 years of work experience; or
Bachelor’s degree in Social Science, International Relations, Regional Studies or a related field from an accredited academic institution with 3 years of relevant work experience.
Experience
Prior Movement Operations, transportation-related and/or management experience are strongly preferred.
Work experience in programs including movement operations component, particularly in resettlement programs.
Experience with guiding and leading junior staff members.
Experience with communicating with beneficiaries.
Skills
Computer/software literate with good knowledge in Microsoft Office.
Strong interpersonal and communication skills.
Attention to detail and ability to organize.
Self-motivated, objective-driven driven and able to use own initiative and work under pressure with minimum supervision.
Team leading skills.

title: Data Architect
description:
Devoteam is a leading consulting firm focused on digital strategy, tech platforms and cybersecurity. By combining creativity, tech and data insights, we empower our customers to transform their business and unlock the future.
With 30 years’ experience and more than 12,000 employees across Europe, the Middle East and Africa, Devoteam promotes responsible tech for people and works to create better change.

Job Description
As a Data Architect, you design, develop, and govern data solutions across the organization.

You work on projects involving data integration, migration, and platform optimization while guaranteeing data quality, security, and scalability.
You advise technical and business teams on best practices, standards, and tools related to data management, including innovative approaches.
You play a key role in promoting architectural standards.
Qualifications
Degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, or a related field.
Strong experience in data architecture, data modeling, and designing complex data systems.
Expertise in Big Data technologies (e.g., Hadoop, Spark), relational databases, and NoSQL.
Proficient with cloud platforms (Azure, AWS, GCP) and modern data management solutions.
Deep knowledge of ETL/ELT processes and data pipeline development.
Experience with AI frameworks and advanced analytics is a plus.
Project management skills and ability to collaborate in multidisciplinary teams.
Excellent communication skills to translate technical concepts to business stakeholders.
Fluent in English is a must have
Additional Information
Opportunity to contribute to a strategic and high-impact data initiative.
Collaborative and innovative work environment.
Professional growth and certification opportunities.

title: (Senior) Manager IT/Cyber Audit (m/f/d)
description:
Application Closing Date: 4th April, 2025 (Please note that due to a high number of applications we might need to close the role ealier than the application closing date - so don't delay!)
Based on a risk based systematic approach to audit assignments, you will be identifying potential risks, recommending mitigation strategies, evaluating control design and operational effectiveness of IT security measures. The Group Audit team provides value and insights to management. There will be a focus on cybersecurity.
About Our Offer
Personal benefits: Attractive remuneration, discretionary bonus schemes, generous travel benefits, extensive health & well-being support, and more
Flexible working: Work is something you do, not somewhere you go. We encourage a healthy work-life balance with a dynamic working environment
A career to shape: Access the TUI Learning Hub to level-up and reach your ambitions
Broaden your network: We champion intercultural collaboration and provide opportunities to work on global projects and teams
Community: Get involved with incredible local charity and sustainability initiatives like the TUI Care Foundation
About The Job
Planning, execution and reporting of allocated audit projects
Identify cybersecurity weaknesses across the group, linking with processes and controls; giving advice for improvement by commercially realistic recommendations
Produce high quality reports for management, that encourage debate over issues and implementation of commercially relevant recommendations
Work on internal continuous improvement projects in an agile team environment
Develop and implement audit methodologies, frameworks, and best practices.
Stay updated on the latest trends and developments in cybersecurity and IT audit.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure the implementation of audit recommendations, networking with the IT security community in TUI and outside; keep contact to relevant stakeholders
Mentor and guide team members across the department, fostering a culture of continuous improvement.
About You
You have a degree in Computer Science, Economics, or a comparable field, and ideally relevant cyber security certifications
You bring experience in internal or external audit of information systems and/or information security processes, or you bring experiences in (cyber) security assessments
Curiosity, analytical thinking, problem-solving skills, and strong communication abilities characterise you
You are keen in continuously learning new requirements and apply your skills in an international and intercultural environment
You are familiar with dealing with stakeholders and you like to drive change
You like to work with an agile and flexible audit approach whilst you like to apply AI and data analytics tools to support you
You are fluent in English; additional languages, German preferred, are an advantage
*From a workplace to a place to belong. At TUI we embrace diversity, equity, and inclusion, encouraging everyone to come as you are, because together, our potential is limitless.*
*We are committed to supporting candidates with disabilities and impairments so if you require any support, please do let us know.*

title: Automation Test Engineer (UFT | Relocation to Portugal)
Senior Network Administrator [F/M/X]
description:
Job Description
As a
Senior Network Administrator
, you are part of the internal IT Operations Department, and you will be responsible for the architecture, security, and administration of the global network infrastructure of Mantu.
You will be responsible for leading the design and maintenance of our multi-Offices connectivity (wired & Wi-Fi), securing our data centers, and acting as a technical mentor for the support team.
As a
Senior Network Administrator
: You will drive the management, evolution and optimization of our network.; You will have the responsibility to overlook our Firewalls, switches, and all other network connectivity devices, and automate recurrent administration tasks to ensure high availability and security.
Your Missions
Manage and modernize global LAN, WAN, and WLAN (WiFi) infrastructure to ensure seamless office connectivity.
Enhance network reliability by implementing Network-as-Code strategies using “Ansible, Terraform & Python”.
Administrate Hybrid Cloud Networking.
Manage Routers, Switches, Firewalls (Palo Alto/Fortinet).
Implement and maintain network monitoring and observability systems (Grafana and Elastick stack) to ensure proactive alerting.
Support, maintain, and optimize the group’s security posture through strict Firewall policies and VPN management.
Resolve complex connectivity incidents (Level 3), and lead root cause analysis for outages.
Create and update network topology maps, High-Level Design (HLD) documentation, and procedures.
Enforce network security standards (802.1x, NAC) and collaborate with the security team on audits.
Your Profile
The ideal candidate should have the following competencies and skills:
5 – 8+ years of experience in a similar position (Network Engineering / Architecture).
Deep knowledge of Routing & Switching protocols (BGP, OSPF, VLANs, STP) on enterprise hardware.
Knowledge of Network Security including Firewalls, IPsec VPNs, and Network Access Control.
Knowledge of Cloud Networking services (Azure / AWS).
Monitoring/logging tools experience (Grafana, Elastik).
Good communication skills in English (oral and written communication).
Teamwork and knowledge sharing (mentoring junior engineers).
Analytical and Problem solving.
Proactive and logical.
Adaptability (work in a continuously changing environment).
Automation logic and confidence with Ansible and Terraform are required, Python is highly valued.
What We Offer
An international community bringing together 110+ different nationalities
An environment where trust has a central place: 70% of our key leaders started their careers at the first level of responsibilities
A robust training system with our internal Academy and 250+ available modules
A vibrant workplace that frequently gathers for internal events (afterworks, team buildings, etc.)
At Mantu, sustainability is part of everything we do. You’ll have the opportunity to turn your ideas into action and make a tangible impact. Every day, our teams bring our ESG commitments to life, from reducing our footprint to driving positive change within our communities. Through our WeCare Together program, you’ll be empowered to design and lead projects that create real social or environmental impact, with the company’s full support.
Mantu is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to promoting diversity within the workforce and creating an inclusive working environment. For this purpose, we welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of gender, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, beliefs, age, marital status, disability, or other characteristics.
Who are we?
Mantu is an independent international consulting player, founded in 2007. Bringing together expert and complementary brands, Mantu stands out for the breadth of its spectrum, responding to all business transformation challenges.
Its activities are divided into four practices:
Leadership & Advocacy, Technology, Brand Strategy & Experiences and Total Talent Management.
A wide range of skills, all serving a single mission: connecting and powering companies with leading teams and technology to succeed faster and sustainably.
From its headquarters in Geneva, Switzerland, Mantu relies on a community of 12,000 talented people in more than 60 countries on 5 continents and has a turnover of 1billion euros.

title: Senior Data Scientist
description:
Devoteam is a leading consulting firm focused on digital strategy, tech platforms and cybersecurity. By combining creativity, tech and data insights, we empower our customers to transform their business and unlock the future.
With 25 years’ experience and more than 11,000 employees across Europe, the Middle East and Africa, Devoteam promotes responsible tech for people and works to create better change.
Creative tech for Better Change.
http://www.devoteam.com
Description Du Poste
We are in search of a Senior Data Scientist boasting at least 3 years of expertise in the fields of Data Science and Machine Learning, with a preference for individuals versed in NLP, Generative AI, Large Language Models (LLMs), MLOps, optimization methods, and AI solution design.
In this capacity, your crucial contribution will be to the crafting and execution of AI solutions, utilizing your advanced technical knowledge.
The successful applicant will possess a good comprehension of AI technology, alongside a proven track record in the development and deployment of innovative AI models and systems.
Additionally, proficiency in data engineering, DevOps, and MLOps methodologies will greatly benefit this position.
Key responsibilities :
Collaborating with client teams to design and implement Generative AI solutions leveraging Machine Learning and Generative AI technologies.
Establishing and advocating for best practices within the community.
Assessing and choosing the right AI tools and machine learning models for specific projects, including the creation and training of these models with Python and other open-source platforms.
Engaging with leadership and key stakeholders to uncover AI opportunities and support strategic development.
Qualifications
Hold a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a similar discipline.
At least 3 years of professional experience in Data Science and Machine Learning.
Profound knowledge of machine learning, deep learning, and generative AI methodologies.
Experience and expertise in the domain of Generative AI.
Skilled in programming with languages like Python, R, and familiar with TensorFlow, PyTorch, or similar frameworks.
A solid grasp of NLP techniques and familiarity with BERT, GPT, or Transformer models.
Experience in computer vision for tasks such as image recognition, object detection, or image generation.
Practical experience with cloud services like Azure or AWS.

Effective collaboration with software development and operational teams to ensure the smooth integration and deployment of AI technologies.
Exceptional problem-solving and analytical abilities, capable of converting business needs into technical strategies.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, able to work efficiently with team members and stakeholders at all levels.

Employ technologies like Docker, Kubernetes, and Git for constructing and overseeing AI workflows.

Work closely with software development and operational teams to ensure smooth integration and deployment of AI models.
Knowledgeable in DevOps and MLOps methodologies, specifically in the areas of continuous integration, deployment, and monitoring of AI models.

This role necessitate mobility flexibility, allowing individuals to work interchangeably between Tunis and Riyadh (Saudi Arabia).

title: IT Functional Lead Engineer.
description:
Vantive is a vital organ therapy company on a mission to extend lives and expand possibilities for patients and care teams everywhere. For 70 years, our team has driven meaningful innovations in kidney care. As we build on our legacy, we are deepening our commitment to elevating the dialysis experience through digital solutions and advanced services, while looking beyond kidney care and investing in transforming vital organ therapies. Greater flexibility and efficiency in therapy administration for care teams, and longer, fuller lives for patients— that is what Vantive aspires to deliver.
We believe Vantive will not only build our leadership in the kidney care space, it will also offer meaningful work to those who join us. At Vantive, you will become part of a community of people who are focused, courageous and don’t settle for the mediocre. Each of us is driven to help improve patients’ lives worldwide. Join us in advancing our mission to extend lives and expand possibilities.
We are seeking a detail-oriented and analytical Engineering Business Analyst to join our team. In this role, you will act as a bridge between technical teams, business stakeholders, and external partners to ensure the successful implementation and optimization of Engineering systems.
IT Functional Lead Engineering perform continuous improvement and support of a set of local/regional Engineering applications that are used across our manufacturing facilities in order to ensure proper solutions for manufacturing business needs accordingly with standards and policies.
Essential Duties And Responsibilities
Link between Engineering business and IT Stakeholders.
Partner with Engineering business, product owners and tech delivery and operations teams to gather, understand, and document business objectives, specifications, and requirements on small/medium size projects.    Responsible for translating business and technical requirements into an architectural blueprint to achieve business goals.
Provide extensive knowledge of application process\integration best practice along with strong written, listening, and verbal communication skills.
Ensuring Data integrity and cybersecurity for the Engineering applications in partnership with the Global team and in alignment of the Company Policies covering the role of system owner
Manage User Acceptance Testing across processes and projects; manage development and execution of unit/system test cases, scripts, and plans.
Support on driving IT efficiencies across the supported application stack by identifying opportunities for application portfolio rationalization to lower IT’s cost to serve while reducing business risks.
Support the compliance of Applications environments constantly collaborating with validation team
Participate in creating an ideal environment in which IT drives a “one IT” mentality and collaborates across its own matrix to provide the best outcomes for our business peers and customers.
Capable to apply blueprints, standards & simplification in order to ensure best solutions are in place to serve Operations & ultimately our customers.
As part of a global team, working internationally is possible mainly in regional concept.
Able to reach effectiveness in understanding requirements and building a functional design.
Other duties may be assigned.
Qualifications
Familiarity with information models and Healthcare industry data standards such as automation and agile architecture
Use professional concepts, and company policies and procedures to solve a variety of problems.
Key skills and abilities include self-motivated, attention to detail, problem solving, presentation, communication, process improvement, an analytical mindset, and team player.
Strong organizational and accountability skills required.
Ability to work effectively in a team environment, independently and under minimal direct supervision.
Having an IT technical understanding focus on the engineering environment.
Ability to manage multiple projects at once.
Ability to work collaboratively with a variety of business and technical stakeholders.
Ability to work in a culture that thrives on feedback and seeks opportunities to stretch outside your comfort zone, with bias for action and completion
Enthusiasm for continuous learning and improvement with unconditional dedication to quality.
Motivation, critical thinking, and problem-solving & collaboration eamwork.
Customer focus.
Education And Experience
Bachelor’s degree with 5+  years of experience as a Engineering business analyst, with a number of projects delivered.
Knowledge of GxP regulations [e.g., 21 CFR (Code of Federal Regulations) Part 11, GAMP 5, MHRA (Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency) etc.], Standards, and Industry Best Practices.
Strong knowledge of implementation processes in an Engineering environment.
Knowledge of SCADA, PLC and other equipment running in a manufacturing environment.
Knowledge of AI, Machine learning and other new technologies
Effective communication skills with the ability to simplify technology for someone not as versed; use shared language.
English language proficiency written and spoken.
Familiarity with Waterfall, Agile and hybrid project management frameworks.

title: Ingénieur connectivité des systèmes industriels (OT Engineer) F/H - SAFRAN ELECTRICAL AND POWER TUNISIA SARL
description:
Safran est un groupe international de haute technologie opérant dans les domaines de l'aéronautique (propulsion, équipements et intérieurs), de l'espace et de la défense. Sa mission : contribuer durablement à un monde plus sûr, où le transport aérien devient toujours plus respectueux de l'environnement, plus confortable et plus accessible. Implanté sur tous les continents, le Groupe emploie 100 000 collaborateurs pour un chiffre d'affaires de 27,3 milliards d'euros en 2024, et occupe, seul ou en partenariat, des positions de premier plan mondial ou européen sur ses marchés.  Safran est la 2ème entreprise du secteur aéronautique et défense du classement « World's Best Companies 2024 » du magazine TIME.  Safran Electrical & Power est l'un des leaders mondiaux des systèmes électriques aéronautiques. La société est un acteur clé dans le domaine de l'électrification des équipements et de la propulsion électrique et hybride. Elle compte 14 000 collaborateurs répartis dans 13 pays.
Descriptif mission
Concevoir, mettre en place, et maintenir des systèmes industriels connectés suivant les règles de cybersécurité, architecture et infrastructures des automatismes et systèmes d'information au niveau société et/ou périmètre équivalent en termes de complexité. Donner aux opérationnels les solutions numériques pour optimiser l'utilisation des moyens industriels (le suivi de l'opération, la maintenance et la réaction en cas de dérive). Piloter et définir les données à capter et le plan de connexion, en veillant à la cohérence des standards groupe et en lien avec les besoins métiers. En amont de l'installation d'une machine, réaliser des pré études afin de conseiller sur la réponse au besoin métier en termes d'automatisme, de connexion, de supervision de machines et de paramétrage (performance, fiabilité, coût, ...). Réaliser le cahier des charges sur son périmètre. Réaliser et/ou optimiser une modélisation des données sous forme d'architecture. Piloter le plan de connexion machine, gérer et proposer des optimisations dans le cadre du pilotage du parc de machines industrielles sur un site de production, en tenant compte des contraintes de cybersécurité, des règles d'architecture et d'infrastructures des systèmes d'information, en collaboration avec les Data Architectes et les métiers associés. Piloter, pour un ensemble de machines, la cartographie des données à capter et le plan de connexion, en veillant à la cohérence des standards OT (systèmes industriels) groupe et des besoins métiers. Proposer et valider des évolutions de fonctionnalité de la connectivité et des automatismes de machine pour répondre aux nouveaux besoins liés à l'industrie 4.0. Définir les outils de supervision des systèmes et des processus (incidents, pannes, ) à mettre en place. Participer à la réalisation de projets industriels au niveau société et/ou périmètre équivalent en termes de complexité en apportant un savoir-faire et tout en garantissant un respect des standards établis (exemples : chantiers d'amélioration continue, obsolescence automatique, création de lignes de production, traitement des modes de connectivité machines, etc.) Suivre les évolutions des technologies matérielles et logicielles pour son périmètre. Déployer et auditer des standards "OT" (systèmes industriels) sur le périmètre industriel. Fournir une documentation pour assister les autres professionnels dans l'utilisation et la maintenance des systèmes.

title: Senior Backend Engineer (Security & FinTech)
description:
We are one of the leading technology groups in Italy. We operate as a Business & Technology Accelerator for companies, financial institutions, and public sector organizations, supporting the evolution of their IT and digital strategies. Through an ecosystem of companies that share values, business strategies, and a common vision, we enable our clients to unlock the full potential of technology, amplifying its positive impact on both the economy and society.
What To Expect
We are looking for a “Security Champion” to join our Product Engineering team. If you are a “FinTech Survivor” who loves strict code reviews and “Zero Trust” environments but is tired of the slow pace of traditional banking, this role is for you. You won’t just be patching vulnerabilities; you will design the secure architecture that protects our customers’ most sensitive financial data.
As a Senior Backend Engineer, you will act as the “Anchor” for our FinTech domain. You will own the server-side logic with a specific mandate: ensure our system is secure by design. You will build the “secure pipes” that deliver data to our AI models, ensuring compliance with federal audits (SOC2, GLBA) without slowing down innovation.
What You Will Do
You will design systems where no component trusts another by default, defining strict encryption standards (at rest/in transit) and enforcing data segregation between tenants.
You will write code that mitigates OWASP Top 10 risks (SQLi, XSS) by design, rather than fixing them after the fact.
You will implement robust Authentication and fine-grained Authorization mechanisms using protocols like OAuth2, OIDC, and RBAC/ABAC.
You are responsible for building immutable logging systems where every API call and database access is traced and searchable to satisfy federal audits.
You will build data anonymization pipelines that strip PII from banking data before it reaches our Data Scientists, ensuring they can work safely.
You will proactively update dependencies and refactor code based on security scanning results (SAST/DAST).
What We Expect From You
5+ years of experience in backend development.
A strong track record in regulated industries (FinTech, HealthTech, Insurance) or cybersecurity engineering.
Proficiency in designing Microservices, Event-Driven architectures, or Modular Monoliths.
Expert skills in API Design (REST, GraphQL, gRPC) and Testing Frameworks.
Deep knowledge of Application Security (OWASP Top 10), Cryptography standards, and Identity Protocols (OAuth2, OIDC, SAML).
Someone used to strict code reviews and handling PII, but looking for a faster startup environment.
A developer who naturally gravitates towards security tickets and always spots the XSS vulnerability in code reviews.
Experience building audit trails for SOC2 or GDPR.

title: Senior Data Scientist
description:
We are one of the leading technology groups in Italy. We operate as a Business & Technology Accelerator for companies, financial institutions, and public sector organizations, supporting the evolution of their IT and digital strategies. Through an ecosystem of companies that share values, business strategies, and a common vision, we enable our clients to unlock the full potential of technology, amplifying its positive impact on both the economy and society.
What To Expect
We are looking for a Data Scientist capable of bridging the gap between vague business questions and rigorous mathematical answers. You must be capable of digging into messy data to find the “truth” and defining what problems should (and can) be solved. You will own the scientific strategy, determining which problems are solvable and proving that our solutions actually work.
As our Senior Data Scientist, you will act as the Strategic Lead of the Applied Science Track. You will report to the Head of Engineering/Principal Scientist and define the roadmap for our Machine Learning, NLP, and Optimization (Operations Research) initiatives.
Crucially, this is a hands-on leadership role. You will be the "Architect" who defines the problem, but you will also actively get your hands dirty with data analysis to validate feasibility. You define the work for the Applied Scientist (Optimization), the Machine Learning Engineer (the Builder), and the NLP Specialist (the Domain Expert). Your goal is to ensure we are solving the right problems with the right data, avoiding the trap of "AI looking for a use case."
What You Will Do
You will collaborate with Product Managers to translate abstract business goals (e.g., "reduce downtime") into concrete mathematical problems (e.g., "predict failure probability," "minimize scheduling cost," or "categorize maintenance logs automatically").
You will dive into large, complex industrial datasets to uncover hidden patterns, trends, and anomalies before any modeling begins.
For Optimization projects, you define the "Business Objective" (e.g., "balance cost vs. speed") that the Applied Scientist will encode into the mathematical solver. You ensure the math solves the actual business need.
You will design and execute experiments (A/B testing, hypothesis testing) to validate that our models are performing better than random chance. You prevent the team from being fooled by noise.
You will build the initial "Proof of Concept" models to test if a solution is possible. Once validated, you hand off the logic to the Engineers/Scientists for production hardening.
You define the "Definition of Success" (accuracy, precision/recall, ROI) that the rest of the technical team must aim for.
What We Expect From You
5+ years of experience in Data Science/Applied Statistics, comfortable working with messy, unstructured data.
Expert in Python and SQL (Pandas, Scikit-Learn, Statsmodels).
Strong statistical foundation (probability, hypothesis testing, causal inference) with sound judgment on when not to use deep learning.
Industrial/MRO exposure, including time-series, sensor data, or logistics.
Experience mentoring/leading a small technical team and collaborating across Operations Research, NLP (LLMs, embeddings, RAG), and Engineering, mindful of latency and cost constraints.

title: Data Scientist
description:
Yassir
is the leading super App in the Maghreb region set to changing the way daily services are provided. It currently operates in
45 cities across Algeria, Morocco and Tunisia with recent expansions into France, Canada and Sub-Saharan Africa
. It is backed (:
$200M in funding
) by VCs from Silicon Valley, Europe and other parts of the world.
We offer on-demand services such as ride-hailing and last-mile delivery. Building on this infrastructure, we are now introducing financial services to help our users pay, save and borrow digitally.
Helping usher the continent into a digital economy era. We’re not just about serving people - we’re about creating a marketplace to bring people what they need while infusing social values.
As data scientist at Yassir, working in the Data and AI team, you will have the opportunity to do life-changing work for Yassir’s millions of customers. You will apply groundbreaking analytics that will shape the super-app strategy of Yassir and support the growth and success of all the services Yassir is currently offering across 45 cities in 7 countries.
Responsibilities:
You will work on different products, including ML/AI products, front-end products and backend systems, and will apply your data analysis skills, your analytical thinking and product intuition to create a marketplace of on-demand services.
You will work cross-functionally with partners across Product, Engineering, Research, Marketing, etc. to identify and solve the most important business and product problems for Yassir.
You are a data ambassador and foster a data-driven product development culture.
Impact: You will influence your partners’ strategy and execution. You will use data to shape product development, optimise our operations, quantify new opportunities, identify upcoming challenges, and ensure the products we build bring value to all parties in our marketplace. You will help your partner teams prioritize what to build, set goals, track and improve day-to-day progress and master their marketplace.
Analytics: You will work with Engineering on collecting the right data to enable data driven decisions at Yassir. You will guide teams using data and insights. You will focus on developing hypotheses for where impact opportunities are and will develop and use a diverse toolkit of rigorous analytical approaches, statistical approaches, different methodologies, frameworks, and technical approaches to test them, building a continuous test-and-learn environment.
Communication and influence: You will tell data-driven stories. You will influence your partners using insights and recommendations and move your organization to being truly and solely data-driven.
Minimum Qualifications
Bachelor's degree in Mathematics, Statistics, a relevant technical field, or equivalent practical experience.
A minimum experience of 2 years in analytics or data science in a business or product environment.
Experience with data querying languages (e.g. SQL), scripting languages (e.g. Python), and/or statistical/mathematical software (e.g. R)
Benefits
Great compensation and bonuses including stock options
Blue sky opportunity with the team; shape the strategic direction of the company
Sharp, motivated co-workers in a fun hybrid environment
Paid company holidays
WHY YOU SHOULD JOIN YASSIR
Join one of the fastest-growing tech companies in Africa
Have a lasting impact on our company's culture
Make a real impact on the world by helping us bring affordable financial and on-demand services to millions of Africans
Work on some really hard technical challenges from identity infrastructure for Africans, digital payment networks to complex mapping and routing systems across the continent
We are the first Algerian startup to go through Y Combinator program and we’re backed by top investors including Unpopular Ventures, Rebel Fund and DainTree.VC
Do you want to become part of our first-class team? You should definitely send us your application. 🚀
At Yassir, we believe in the power of diversity and the importance of an inclusive culture. So, if you're ready to bring your unique perspective and experiences to the table, then we're excited to listen.
Don't just apply for a job, come and be a part of our journey. Let's create a better tomorrow together.
We look forward to receiving your application!
Best of luck,
Your Yassir TA Team
We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Senior Generative AI (Gen AI) Engineer
description:
Senior Generative AI (GenAI) Engineer
Description
InnovationTeam
is a forward-thinking technology company that specializes in delivering advanced AI-driven solutions to enterprises and government organizations. We are currently seeking a highly skilled Senior Generative AI (GenAI) Engineer to join our AI team.
As a Senior GenAI Engineer at
InnovationTeam
, you will be responsible for designing, developing, and deploying large-scale generative AI solutions. You will work closely with cross-functional teams to translate business and technical requirements into robust AI systems and bring them into production.
This role requires deep expertise in modern AI architectures, large language models, and production-ready software engineering practices. The ideal candidate is highly motivated, detail-oriented, and capable of owning AI solutions end to end.
At
InnovationTeam
, we value innovation, collaboration, and continuous learning. We provide a flexible and inclusive work environment where your expertise is recognized and opportunities for professional growth are continuously supported.
Role Overview
We are seeking a skilled AI Engineer with a strong focus on Generative AI technologies to develop, optimize, and deploy AI Agents and solutions. The role involves working on real-world use cases and data analytics problems and delivering production-ready AI systems.
Key Responsibilities
Design, develop, and optimize Generative AI solutions (LLMs, RAG systems, AI agents)
Fine-tune, evaluate, and deploy large language models for enterprise use cases
Build and maintain end-to-end AI pipelines, from data ingestion to inference
Implement Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) using vector databases and knowledge stores
Integrate GenAI solutions with existing systems via APIs and microservices
Optimize model performance, latency, and cost on cloud platforms (OCI, AWS, Azure, or GCP)
Collaborate with product, data, and engineering teams to translate business needs into AI solutions
Ensure AI solutions follow best practices in security, governance, and responsible AI
Stay up to date with the latest developments in GenAI, LLMs, and AI tooling
Required Skills & Qualifications
Minimum 5 years of professional experience in AI / Machine Learning
Master's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, or a related field
Proficiency in programming languages such as python , Java , and Ruby
Strong experience with Large Language Models (LLMs) (e.g., GPT, LLaMA, Mistral, etc.)
Hands-on experience with Python and AI/ML frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow)
Solid understanding of NLP, deep learning, and transformer architectures
Experience with RAG architectures, vector databases (FAISS, OpenSearch, Pinecone, etc.)
Strong software engineering skills (APIs, REST, microservices, Docker, Kubernetes)
Experience deploying AI workloads on cloud platforms (OCI preferred)
Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills
Excellent command of English (written and spoken)
Nice to Have
Experience with GPU-based training and inference
Familiarity with MLOps / LLMOps practices
Experience in regulated or enterprise environments
Knowledge of AI governance, ethics, and compliance frameworks
What We Offer
Opportunity to work on cutting-edge Generative AI projects
Collaborative, innovation-driven environment
Competitive compensation package
Access to modern AI infrastructure and cloud resources
Professional growth and learning opportunities

title: AI Engineer [F/M/X]
description:

Join our Data & AI team and start a new adventure in an international and dynamic environment, where you will be able to fulfill your career expectations in a fast-growing organization. Positioned at the heart of our evolution, our Data & AI team is pivotal in driving both team and organizational growth. As a,
AI Engineer
, you will play a key role in developing and refining machine learning models, building robust deployment pipelines, and collaborating with talented colleagues across the organization to deliver impactful solutions in a dynamic, innovative environment.
Your missions:
Contribute to the development, optimization, and deployment of machine learning models and pipelines.
Build and maintain CI/CD processes for model training and deployment.
Deploy AI Agents in production with Langchain/Langgraph and MCP/Skills
Knows the difference between vibe coding and agentic coding via Claude Code or Cursor

Participate in the collection, cleaning, and preprocessing of large-scale datasets.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand business challenges and provide data-driven insights.

Document findings, experiments, and best practices, and present recommendations to stakeholders.

Degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Engineering, or a related field.
Deep understanding of machine learning concepts and algorithms.
Proficiency in Python for data science and machine learning (e.g., pandas, numpy, seaborn, matplotlib).
Experience with OpenAI, HuggingFace, PyTorch and/or TensorFlow, Flask and/or FastAPI.
Proficiency in Docker, SQL and NoSQL databases, and CI/CD processes.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail.
Excellent communication and teamwork abilities.
Eagerness to take on challenging tasks.

An international community bringing together 110+ different nationalities

Mantu is an independent international consulting player, founded in 2007. Bringing together expert and complementary brands, Mantu stands out for the breadth of its spectrum, responding to all business transformation challenges.

A wide range of skills, all serving a single mission: connecting and powering companies with leading teams and technology to succeed faster and sustainably.

title: AI Video Marketing Internship
description:
About M365Consult
M365Consult is a fast-growing European Microsoft 365 consultancy specializing in digital transformation, cybersecurity, and AI-driven solutions for clients across the DACH region (Germany, Austria, Switzerland). Our team is passionate about innovation, collaboration, and building a strong brand presence across digital platforms. We are looking for motivated individuals eager to learn, grow, and help us communicate complex Microsoft topics through engaging video content.
To expand our brand and accelerate our reach, we are launching a remote internship for a motivated AI Video Marketing Intern, someone eager to learn how to create viral video content that speaks to both Microsoft experts and the organizations working with M365Consult on their cloud and security journey.
What This Internship Is About
This is not just about making videos; it is about building scalable, viral content systems. You will learn how to plan, generate, and scale video content using cutting-edge AI tools, collaborate with consultants and automation engineers, and simulate processes that will power the video content engine of a Microsoft-focused consulting brand.
You will gain hands-on experience with leading AI video tools and be part of shaping M365Consults' visual voice across:
YouTube Shorts and Instagram Reels
TikTok-style viral content
LinkedIn video posts and carousels
Client testimonial and success-story videos
Product and solution demo videos (Microsoft 365, security, Azure, AI)
Educational explainer content for business and IT audiences

title: Development Intern
description:
Infor Decision Analytics and Science (IDEAS) team is responsible for setting the AI/ML strategy at Infor. Our industry-specific Augmented Intelligence (AI) solutions suite in Asset, Forecast, Inventory, Customer, Pricing, Sales, Data and Operational Intelligence domains provides rapid and measurable value to enterprise customers. In pursuit of advancing our AI solutions, IDEAS is currently seeking a Development Intern - Machine Learning (ML).
This internship will center on advancing IDEAS’ Forecasting solution by developing modern machine-learning-driven forecasting methods capable of handling lumpy, highly irregular, and intermittent demand patterns a key challenge in real-world supply chain and inventory planning.
A Day in The Life Typically Includes:
* Exploring a new frontier in forecasting, beginning with a systematic evaluation of foundational time-series approaches, including classical statistical models, machine-learning methods, and intermittent-demand techniques such as Croston, SBA, and TSB.
* Investigating advanced deep-learning architectures to model lumpy demand, including LSTM-based networks and state-of-the-art transformer models purpose-built for time series forecasting (e.g., Temporal Fusion Transformer, Informer, PatchTST).
* Evaluating the potential of large language models (LLMs) to enhance forecasting pipelines whether through feature generation, metadata understanding, demand-pattern classification, or hybrid architectures that combine semantic information with historical numerical data.
* Comparing all approaches using benchmark datasets to determine how these emerging models perform relative to the current Infor TS Forecasting Engine.
* Prototyping integrations into our existing architecture and identifying opportunities to strengthen Infor’s AI-driven forecasting capabilities across diverse business domains.
* Maintaining development artifacts and technical documentation in GitLab, ensuring reproducibility, traceability, and alignment with best engineering practices.
Basic Qualifications:
* Final year candidates in the Master's / Engineering program in Statistics, Mathematics, Physics, Computer Science, Operations Research, or a related quantitative discipline from an accredited university.
* Software development experience in Python and familiarity with scientific libraries commonly used in statistical analysis and machine learning e.g., pytorch, transformers library, and scikit-learn. Familiarity and basic understanding of PySpark, SQL or other data processing frameworks.
* Familiarity with software development environments such as Visual Studio Code, and version control using Git and CI/CD pipelines.
* Knowledge of data models, graphs, and relational databases. Proficiency in handling large datasets and data analysis with Tableau, Power BI, or similar BI tools.
Preferred Qualifications:
* Knowledge of core machine learning techniques.
* Knowledge of data models and relational databases. Experience handling large datasets and data analysis with BI tools.
* Familiarity with task management with JIRA.

Join a global community of bold thinkers and innovators, where your expertise doesn’t just solve problems. it shapes industries, unlocks opportunities, and creates real-world impact for billions of people. title: Data engineer
description:
With its beginnings in a family run pharmacy in Correggio, Italy in the 1920s, Recordati is now a global pharmaceutical company, listed on the Italian stock exchange, with over 4,500 employees and turnover of over Euro 2bn.
We are a group of like-minded, passionate individuals who go to extraordinary lengths for our patients, customers, partners, investors and the people across the globe who we serve. We develop and commercialise medicines to serve people living with common diseases, as well as those living with some of the rarest, in around 150 countries.
At Recordati, our mantra is simple. We’ve always believed that health, and the opportunity to live life to the fullest, is a right, not a privilege. Whether that is for common diseases or the rarest – we want to give people the opportunity to be the best version of themselves.
This drive will never stop. Together, we will always be reimagining tomorrow – with new ideas, new technologies and new innovations to fight diseases.
Recordati. Unlocking the full potential of life.

traçabilité réglementaire

BPF/GMP
.

Assurer la documentation technique et fonctionnelle.

title: Senior Data Scientist
description:
We are one of the leading technology groups in Italy. We operate as a Business & Technology Accelerator for companies, financial institutions, and public sector organizations, supporting the evolution of their IT and digital strategies. Through an ecosystem of companies that share values, business strategies, and a common vision, we enable our clients to unlock the full potential of technology, amplifying its positive impact on both the economy and society.
What To Expect
We are looking for a Data Scientist capable of bridging the gap between vague business questions and rigorous mathematical answers. You must be capable of digging into messy data to find the “truth” and defining what problems should (and can) be solved. You will own the scientific strategy, determining which problems are solvable and proving that our solutions actually work.
As our Senior Data Scientist, you will act as the Strategic Lead of the Applied Science Track. You will report to the Head of Engineering/Principal Scientist and define the roadmap for our Machine Learning, NLP, and Optimization (Operations Research) initiatives.
Crucially, this is a hands-on leadership role. You will be the "Architect" who defines the problem, but you will also actively get your hands dirty with data analysis to validate feasibility. You define the work for the Applied Scientist (Optimization), the Machine Learning Engineer (the Builder), and the NLP Specialist (the Domain Expert). Your goal is to ensure we are solving the right problems with the right data, avoiding the trap of "AI looking for a use case."
What You Will Do
You will collaborate with Product Managers to translate abstract business goals (e.g., "reduce downtime") into concrete mathematical problems (e.g., "predict failure probability," "minimize scheduling cost," or "categorize maintenance logs automatically").
You will dive into large, complex industrial datasets to uncover hidden patterns, trends, and anomalies before any modeling begins.
For Optimization projects, you define the "Business Objective" (e.g., "balance cost vs. speed") that the Applied Scientist will encode into the mathematical solver. You ensure the math solves the actual business need.
You will design and execute experiments (A/B testing, hypothesis testing) to validate that our models are performing better than random chance. You prevent the team from being fooled by noise.
You will build the initial "Proof of Concept" models to test if a solution is possible. Once validated, you hand off the logic to the Engineers/Scientists for production hardening.
You define the "Definition of Success" (accuracy, precision/recall, ROI) that the rest of the technical team must aim for.
What We Expect From You
5+ years of experience in Data Science/Applied Statistics, comfortable working with messy, unstructured data.
Expert in Python and SQL (Pandas, Scikit-Learn, Statsmodels).
Strong statistical foundation (probability, hypothesis testing, causal inference) with sound judgment on when not to use deep learning.
Industrial/MRO exposure, including time-series, sensor data, or logistics.
Experience mentoring/leading a small technical team and collaborating across Operations Research, NLP (LLMs, embeddings, RAG), and Engineering, mindful of latency and cost constraints.

description:
Vantive is a vital organ therapy company on a mission to extend lives and expand possibilities for patients and care teams everywhere. For 70 years, our team has driven meaningful innovations in kidney care. As we build on our legacy, we are deepening our commitment to elevating the dialysis experience through digital solutions and advanced services, while looking beyond kidney care and investing in transforming vital organ therapies. Greater flexibility and efficiency in therapy administration for care teams, and longer, fuller lives for patients— that is what Vantive aspires to deliver.
We believe Vantive will not only build our leadership in the kidney care space, it will also offer meaningful work to those who join us. At Vantive, you will become part of a community of people who are focused, courageous and don’t settle for the mediocre. Each of us is driven to help improve patients’ lives worldwide. Join us in advancing our mission to extend lives and expand possibilities.
We are seeking a detail-oriented and analytical Engineering Business Analyst to join our team. In this role, you will act as a bridge between technical teams, business stakeholders, and external partners to ensure the successful implementation and optimization of Engineering systems.

Link between Engineering business and IT Stakeholders.

Manage User Acceptance Testing across processes and projects; manage development and execution of unit/system test cases, scripts, and plans.

Support the compliance of Applications environments constantly collaborating with validation team
Participate in creating an ideal environment in which IT drives a “one IT” mentality and collaborates across its own matrix to provide the best outcomes for our business peers and customers.
Capable to apply blueprints, standards & simplification in order to ensure best solutions are in place to serve Operations & ultimately our customers.

Alors rejoignez-nous !
Amaris Consulting s’engage à promouvoir la diversité au sein de ses effectifs et à créer un environnement de travail inclusif. Who are we?
Amaris Consulting
is an independent technology consulting firm providing guidance and solutions to businesses. With more than 1,000 clients across the globe, we have been rolling out solutions in major projects for over a decade – this is made possible by an international team of 7,600 people spread across 5 continents and more than 60 countries. We’re focused on building and nurturing a top talent community where all our team members can achieve their full potential. Amaris is your steppingstone to cross rivers of change, meet challenges and achieve all your projects with success.
At Amaris, we strive to provide our candidates with the best possible recruitment experience. We like to get to know our candidates, challenge them, and be able to give them proper feedback as quickly as possible. Here's what our recruitment process looks like:
Brief Call
: Our process typically begins with a brief virtual/phone conversation to get to know you! The objective? Learn about you, understand your motivations, and make sure we have the right job for you!
Interviews
(the average number of interviews is 3 - the number may vary depending on the level of seniority required for the position). During the interviews, you will meet people from our team: your line manager of course, but also other people related to your future role. We will talk in depth about you, your experience, and skills, but also about the position and what will be expected of you. Of course, you will also get to know Amaris: our culture, our roots, our teams, and your career opportunities!
Case study
: Depending on the position, we may ask you to take a test. This could be a role play, a technical assessment, a problem-solving scenario, etc.
As you know, every person is different and so is every role in a company. That is why we have to adapt accordingly, and the process may differ slightly at times. However, please know that we always put ourselves in the candidate's shoes to ensure they have the best possible experience.
We look forward to meeting you!
